value.Title: April 28, 2026: Swinerton to Lead Construction of San Francisco Fire Department Training Campus in $145M Deal

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHR-F9P3-SCRX-631R-00000-00April 28, 2026: Swinerton to Lead Construction of San Francisco Fire Department Training Campus in $145M Deal2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:40:07ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSApril 28, 2026: Swinerton to Lead Construction of San Francisco Fire Department Training Campus in $145M Deal

Swinerton Builders has been named Construction Manager/General Contractor for a $145 million San Francisco Fire Department training facility, with the contract awarded by San Francisco Public Works. Groundbreaking for the project occurred on 16th April 2026.

The new SFFD Division of Training campus will be built in the city's southeastern district and will include a 50,000-square-foot scenario district that replicates the characteristics of local neighborhoods.

Beyond the scenario district, the center will provide emergency medical service training, onsite fire apparatus training, and Neighborhood Emergency Response Team training sessions to prepare responders for a range of incidents.

The Administration/Classroom Building will be designated as an Essential Services Building to ensure immediate occupancy after a major earthquake and will function as a backup emergency operations center.

Christina Pantera, project executive at Swinerton, noted the firm's extensive San Francisco portfolio, while Paul Hinz, vice president and division manager, emphasized the center's role in strengthening community resilience and safety through close collaboration with city stakeholders. Swinerton's 135-year history underpins its capacity to deliver such a facility.

INDEX

SECTION 2 SWINERTON INCORPORATED TOP MANAGEMENT

SECTION 1 SWINERTON INCORPORATED PROFILE

1.1 ACTIVITIES

Swinerton Incorporated provides commercial construction and construction management services in the Western United States. It offers general construction, modernization and renovation, management and consulting, preconstruction, corporate, and renewable energy services. The companys projects include aviation, cultural, education, federal, gaming and entertainment, healthcare, hospitality, interiors, mission critical, mixed-use/residential, office, renewable energy, retail, and special. Swinerton Incorporated was formerly known as Swinerton & Walberg Co. The company was founded in 1888 and is based in San Francisco, California with additional offices in California, Colorado, Hawaii, Texas, New Mexico, and Washington.

urn:contentItem:6JHJ-92P3-RWC8-P4DD-00000-00CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC - INDUSTRY MONITOR: TRENDS AND PEER UPDATES2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:40:07ZLexisNexisSTOCKCLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC - INDUSTRY MONITOR: TRENDS AND PEER UPDATES

DASHBOARD: CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC

PermID4296619307Websitehttp://www.clarkconstruction.comIndustryConstructionAddress18201 Von Karman Avenue Suite 800 Irvine, California 92612

ACTIVITIES:

Clark Construction Group, LLC, founded in 1906, is a leading general contractor headquartered in Bethesda, Maryland. With over 4,000 employees across 20+ offices, it serves commercial, infrastructure, and institutional sectors, delivering projects like stadiums, hospitals, and transit systems. Generating over $5 billion annually, Clark is known for its use of Building Information Modeling (BIM) and sustainable practices. In 2024, it completed major infrastructure projects, emphasizing green construction. Its culture prioritizes safety, diversity, and employee development, earning industry accolades. Competing with Turner Construction, Clark stands out for its large-scale project expertise and client partnerships. Recent initiatives include expanding renewable energy construction and digital tools for project management.

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC [4 Press Releases]

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC GREEN FROM THE INSIDE OUT: BERKELEY LAB SETS NEW STANDARDS

Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory's Biological and Environmental Program Integration Center sets new sustainability standards. Berkeley Lab Biological and Environmental Program Integration Center (BioEPIC) unites scientists from LBNL's biosciences and earth and environmental sciences areas in a four-story, 71,000-square-foot facility that sets a new standard for sustainability. Completed in March, the facility offers a suite of next-generation tools designed to study how microbial communities interact with plants and soil to influence the environment.

Inside, 200 researchers perform carefully controlled experiments on microbe-soil-plant interactions with instruments and computing infrastructure that connect BioEPIC to its field research sites. These unique climate research environments, along with three additional levels of labs and collaborative spaces, are housed in an all-electric facility designed to maximize energy efficiency and resiliency.

By supporting an array of unique research needs in this highly sustainable setting, the BioEPIC

Source: Company Website

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC BUILDERS AT HEART: WINSTON VAN KEULEN

This article is part of our series Builders at Heart, where we highlight the passions and backgrounds of the Clark team - the things that shape us - that allow us to tackle challenges head-on, solve complex problems, and build what matters.

Meet Winston van Keulen, a superintendent with Clark Construction, currently working in San Diego. Discover how he is progressing in his role and the keys to success he shares with new team members.

Source: Company Website

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC FROM CONCEPT TO REALITY: HARNESSING 3D PRINTING AT CLARK'S MID-ATLANTIC YARD

Clark Construction's Mid-Atlantic Yard in Upper Marlboro, Maryland, is a powerhouse of specialized services, from fleet oversight to custom fabrication. This diverse facility is also part of a transformative initiative: the integration of 3D printing for prototyping innovative custom solutions to solve complex challenges.

The method came to life when faced with a challenge: designing a custom cleaning piece for a drill rig auger. While the traditional route would involve costly and time-consuming steel fabrication for a prototype, Charles Harman III, a craft and machine shop supervisor, and his team explored a new solution for this type of fabrication and successfully used a 3D printer to create a scaled model. The successful prototype demonstrated the efficacy of the system, and once the 3D printed model was approved, the team began assembling the steel-fabricated version.

Source: Company Website

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC ENHANCING RICHMOND'S WATER RESILIENCE

Clark Water is currently leading improvements on the Shockoe Diversion Structure in Richmond - a key part of the city's combined sewer overflow (CSO) system, which was originally built in the early 1970s. The project will alleviate sewage and stormwater overflow during high-water events. To achieve this, crews have installed mechanical trash rake screens to prevent debris from passing through, two crest gates to minimize upstream flooding, and are making upgrades to supporting infrastructure.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ TURNER SELECTED FOR $500 MILLION WESTCOURT ORLANDO MIXED-USE DEVELOPMENT

The 900,000-square-foot project developed by JMA Ventures will create a vibrant destination in downtown Orlando.

Source: Company Website

+ TURNER PROVIDES AI-POWERED SAFETY TOOL TO THE CONSTRUCTION INDUSTRY

Turner is making SafeT Coach, its AI-powered field safety tool, available free of charge to the broader construction industry. The announcement comes during Turner's 22nd annual Safety Week and company-wide Safety Stand-Down, it's largest to date, under the theme "All In Together."

In construction, conditions change frequently, and the traditional safety resources that establish standards-manuals, scheduled training, retrospective reporting-are not always accessible when and where field teams need them most. SafeT Coach was developed to close that gap.

Source: Company Website

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION: BUILDING A SUSTAINABLE FUTURE TOGETHER

Throughout April, including Earth Day on April 22, Turner International Turkiye celebrated Earth Month across all 8 workplaces with a series of inspiring activities focused on sustainability and environmental awareness.

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHM-XJ53-RSYN-92RP-00000-00PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP - RECENT INSIGHTS: SECTOR TRENDS & NEWS DEVELOPMENTS2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:40:07ZLexisNexisSTOCKPILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP - RECENT INSIGHTS: SECTOR TRENDS & NEWS DEVELOPMENTS

DASHBOARD: PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP

PermID4296383614Websitewww.pillsburylaw.com/en/IndustryFull-Service BigLawAddress31 West 52nd Street, New York, NY 10019 USA

ACTIVITIES:

Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman LLP, founded in 1868, is a global law firm headquartered in New York City. With over 700 lawyers across 20 offices, it serves clients in energy, technology, and finance, specializing in M&A, regulatory, and litigation. Generating over $700 million annually, Pillsbury advised on 2024 renewable energy transactions, recognized by Columbia Law School. Its culture emphasizes innovation, diversity, and pro bono work, with strong associate training programs. Competing with Baker McKenzie, Pillsbury stands out for its energy and tech regulatory expertise. In 2024, it expanded its AI and ESG practices, focusing on sustainable finance. Recent initiatives include cloud-based legal tech and partnerships with nonprofits like Lawyers Alliance for New York, reinforcing its community impact.

+ PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP [3 Press Releases]

+ PILLSBURY OPENS IN BOSTON WITH A FOCUS ON TECHNOLOGY, LIFE SCIENCES AND FINANCIAL INDUSTRIES

Pillsbury is opening a new office in Boston, marking an important expansion into one of the nation's leading growth and innovation centers. The office will launch with a team of five senior attorneys and additional associates, who bring diverse transactional and litigation backgrounds.

Source: Company Website

+ PILLSBURYS MARQUEE INSURANCE RECOVERY & ADVISORY PRACTICE CONTINUES EXPANSION, WELCOMING RENOWNED POLICYHOLDER LITIGATOR BOB GILBERT

Robert J. Gilbert, a nationally recognized litigator who represents policyholders in complex insurance recovery matters, has joined Pillsbury from Latham & Watkins. He comes to the firm as one of the founding partners of Pillsbury's newly established Boston office.

Source: Company Website

+ AS NOVEL DIGITAL RIGHTS ISSUES COME TO THE FORE, PILLSBURY ADDS IP LITIGATOR ROBERT L. URIARTE IN LOS ANGELES

Pillsbury has further enhanced its expansive tech-focused capabilities with the addition of Intellectual Property litigator and trial lawyer Robert L. Uriarte as a partner in its Los Angeles office.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ PERKINS COIE [3 Press Releases]

+ PERKINS COIE REPRESENTS SEDRON IN INVESTMENT FROM ARA PARTNERS OF UP TO $500 MILLION

PORTLAND, OR (April 15, 2026)-Perkins Coie announced today that it represented Sedron Technologies, a leader in advanced upcycling systems that transform waste streams into reusable water and fertilizer products, in an investment of up to $500 million from Ara Partners, a global private equity and infrastructure firm focused on industrial decarbonization.

Source: Company Website

+ REED SMITH ADVISES LEON CAPITAL ON STRATEGIC INVESTMENT IN AVANTIS GROUP

LONDON - Global law firm Reed Smith has advised funds advised by Leon Capital on the strategic investment in Avantis Group, a UK-based provider of specialist engineering services to the maritime and energy industries. The transaction, which represents the latest new investment by Leon Capital, completed on 23 December 2025. The terms of the investment are confidential.

The transaction involved the Development Bank of Wales and Avantis Group's founders, Chris David and Tom David. Avantis Group's existing management team will retain operational control of the business. Blake Morgan acted as legal counsel to the Development Bank of Wales, while Acuity Law advised the founders. AMA Capital Partners acted as corporate finance adviser to Avantis Group.

Reed Smith advised Leon Capital on all aspects of the transaction, providing comprehensive M&A support in connection with the strategic investment. The matter involved a multi-disciplinary team spanning M&A, tax, and regulatory considerations.

The investment supports Avantis Group's long-term growth strategy across maritime and energy markets, including expansion into digital infrastructure and defence, and reflects Leon Capital's confidence in the company's differentiated engineering platform and experienced management team.

For Reed Smith, the transaction represents a further example of the firm's work advising private capital sponsors on complex M&A transactions and continues to strengthen its relationship with Leon Capital as it expands its platform.

The Reed Smith team advising on the matter was led by Andrew Houghton, with support from Ciaran Sweeney, Georgia Moorhouse and Selina Cook on M&A matters; Caspar Fox and Tom Baxter on tax; and Emma Weeden on FDI and antitrust.

Source: Company Website

+ REED SMITH STRENGTHENS ASIA-PACIFIC FINANCE AND TRANSPORTATION CAPABILITY WITH HIRE IN SINGAPORE

SINGAPORE - Global law firm Reed Smith announced the appointment of banking and finance partner Dr. Gregory Xu to its Singapore office. Xu, who joins from Stephenson Harwood, where he led the general banking and project finance offering in Southeast Asia and served on the firm's Supervisory Council, its executive committee, will join Reed Smith's Transportation Industry Group.

With over 15 years of experience, Xu advises on high-value, cross-border transactions across a wide range of sectors, including transportation - shipping, aviation, and logistics - trade, life sciences, energy, and technology. He regularly acts for financial institutions, funds, export credit agencies, leasing houses, asset owners, and Fortune 500 companies on complex financing, restructuring, and enforcement matters.

Xu has a strong track record in green and sustainability-linked lending and advises on multi-jurisdictional insolvency-related enforcement and restructuring matters. He also brings extensive experience in general corporate and commercial matters.

Richard Hakes, chair of Reed Smith's Transportation Industry Group, said: "Gregory is an outstanding addition to our team, bringing deep knowledge of ship finance, aviation finance, and banking throughout Southeast Asia. His arrival further strengthens our capabilities in Singapore and reinforces our commitment to the maritime, aviation, and broader transportation sectors across the Asia-Pacific region and beyond. Working closely with our shipping teams in Hong Kong and Shanghai and our aviation team in Singapore as part of our global Transportation Industry Group, Gregory will enhance our ability to provide seamless, cross-border support to clients operating in one of the world's most dynamic industries."

Xu commented: "Reed Smith's global platform, sector focus, and collaborative culture are hugely impressive. The firm's market-leading finance practice and its long-standing reputation in general banking, asset finance, and project finance were major draws for me. I'm delighted to be joining such a highly collegiate team with a clear strategy for growth in the region and look forward to contributing to the continued expansion of the firm's finance offering across Asia-Pacific."

Praj Samant, Reed Smith's Asia-Pacific managing partner, said: "We are delighted to welcome Gregory to the Singapore team. His arrival further strengthens our offering in one of the region's most strategically important sectors. We look forward to the valuable contributions he will bring to the firm locally and globally."

Xu is recognized as a leading practitioner by multiple legal directories. Chambers Asia-Pacific ranks him as a "Leading Individual" for Banking & Finance (India), while The Legal 500 identifies him as a "Leading Partner," "Next Generation Partner," and "Recommended" lawyer across Banking & Finance, Asset Finance, Projects, and Shipping in Singapore and in the foreign juris

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHV-R103-SD9Y-N24B-00000-00CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC - SECTOR INSIGHT FROM PEERS2026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:40:07ZLexisNexisSTOCKCLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC - SECTOR INSIGHT FROM PEERS

DASHBOARD: CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC

PermID4296619307Websitehttp://www.clarkconstruction.comIndustryConstructionAddress18201 Von Karman Avenue Suite 800 Irvine, California 92612

ACTIVITIES:

Clark Construction Group, LLC, founded in 1906, is a leading general contractor headquartered in Bethesda, Maryland. With over 4,000 employees across 20+ offices, it serves commercial, infrastructure, and institutional sectors, delivering projects like stadiums, hospitals, and transit systems. Generating over $5 billion annually, Clark is known for its use of Building Information Modeling (BIM) and sustainable practices. In 2024, it completed major infrastructure projects, emphasizing green construction. Its culture prioritizes safety, diversity, and employee development, earning industry accolades. Competing with Turner Construction, Clark stands out for its large-scale project expertise and client partnerships. Recent initiatives include expanding renewable energy construction and digital tools for project management.

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC [4 Press Releases]

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC GREEN FROM THE INSIDE OUT: BERKELEY LAB SETS NEW STANDARDS

Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory's Biological and Environmental Program Integration Center sets new sustainability standards. Berkeley Lab Biological and Environmental Program Integration Center (BioEPIC) unites scientists from LBNL's biosciences and earth and environmental sciences areas in a four-story, 71,000-square-foot facility that sets a new standard for sustainability. Completed in March, the facility offers a suite of next-generation tools designed to study how microbial communities interact with plants and soil to influence the environment.

Inside, 200 researchers perform carefully controlled experiments on microbe-soil-plant interactions with instruments and computing infrastructure that connect BioEPIC to its field research sites. These unique climate research environments, along with three additional levels of labs and collaborative spaces, are housed in an all-electric facility designed to maximize energy efficiency and resiliency.

By supporting an array of unique research needs in this highly sustainable setting, the BioEPIC

Source: Company Website

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC BUILDERS AT HEART: WINSTON VAN KEULEN

This article is part of our series Builders at Heart, where we highlight the passions and backgrounds of the Clark team - the things that shape us - that allow us to tackle challenges head-on, solve complex problems, and build what matters.

Meet Winston van Keulen, a superintendent with Clark Construction, currently working in San Diego. Discover how he is progressing in his role and the keys to success he shares with new team members.

Source: Company Website

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC FROM CONCEPT TO REALITY: HARNESSING 3D PRINTING AT CLARK'S MID-ATLANTIC YARD

Clark Construction's Mid-Atlantic Yard in Upper Marlboro, Maryland, is a powerhouse of specialized services, from fleet oversight to custom fabrication. This diverse facility is also part of a transformative initiative: the integration of 3D printing for prototyping innovative custom solutions to solve complex challenges.

The method came to life when faced with a challenge: designing a custom cleaning piece for a drill rig auger. While the traditional route would involve costly and time-consuming steel fabrication for a prototype, Charles Harman III, a craft and machine shop supervisor, and his team explored a new solution for this type of fabrication and successfully used a 3D printer to create a scaled model. The successful prototype demonstrated the efficacy of the system, and once the 3D printed model was approved, the team began assembling the steel-fabricated version.

Source: Company Website

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC ENHANCING RICHMOND'S WATER RESILIENCE

Clark Water is currently leading improvements on the Shockoe Diversion Structure in Richmond - a key part of the city's combined sewer overflow (CSO) system, which was originally built in the early 1970s. The project will alleviate sewage and stormwater overflow during high-water events. To achieve this, crews have installed mechanical trash rake screens to prevent debris from passing through, two crest gates to minimize upstream flooding, and are making upgrades to supporting infrastructure.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION [1 Press Release]

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION LABOR SHORTAGE LOOMS AS CONSTRUCTION REBOUNDS

SANTA CLARA, Calif., Dec. 9, 2025 - Just as individual market sectors are finding ways to move projects forward after a year of supply chain and cost turbulence, the ongoing labor shortage threatens to bog down construction unless owners and contractors embrace new approaches, according to the latest Market Conditions Report from DPR Construction, one of the nation's top technical builders.

A recent survey by the Associated General Contractors of America and the national Center for Construction Education and Research showed that:

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION [4 Press Releases]

+ TURNER MARKS 124TH ANNIVERSARY WITH FOUNDERS' MONTH OF COMMUNITY IMPACT

In celebration of its founding in May 1902, Turner Construction Company is proud to launch its annual Founders' Month, a global effort that brings together more than 16,000 employees in service to the communities where they live and work.

The construction industry hired approximately four million workers in the United States but added only approximately 400,000 jobs in 2025. The Associated Builders and Contractors estimates the industry will need to attract roughly 365,000 net additional workers in 2026 to keep pace with demand, with skilled trades availability emerging as one of the most critical constraints on project delivery.

With that challenge in mind, this year's Founders' Month places an emphasis on strengthening the construction workforce pipeline - connecting community engagement with training, access, and career opportunities that help bring more people into the trades and support them in building long-term careers.

Source: Company Website

+ TURNER SELECTED FOR $500 MILLION WESTCOURT ORLANDO MIXED-USE DEVELOPMENT

The 900,000-square-foot project developed by JMA Ventures will create a vibrant destination in downtown Orlando.

Source: Company Website

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION: BUILDING A SUSTAINABLE FUTURE TOGETHER

Throughout April, including Earth Day on April 22, Turner International Turkiye celebrated Earth Month across all 8 workplaces with a series of inspiring activities focused on sustainability and environmental awareness.

Source: Company Website

+ TURNER PROVIDES AI-POWERED SAFETY TOOL TO THE CONSTRUCTION INDUSTRY

Turner is making SafeT Coach, its AI-powered field safety tool, available free of charge to the broader construction industry. The announcement comes during Turner's 22nd annual Safety Week and company-wide Safety Stand-Down, it's largest to date, under the theme "All In Together."

In construction, conditions change frequently, and the traditional safety resources that establish standards-manuals, scheduled training, retrospective reporting-are not always accessible when and where field teams need them most. SafeT Coach was developed to close that gap.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

value.Title: Wisconsin candidate for governor Francesca Hong proposes limitations on AI in the workplace

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JH6-0NJ3-SHTX-R47B-00000-00Wisconsin candidate for governor Francesca Hong proposes limitations on AI in the workplace2026-05-04T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:40:07ZLexisNexisLAKEGENEVANEWS.NETWisconsin candidate for governor Francesca Hong proposes limitations on AI in the workplaceKIMBERLY WETHAL kwethal@madison.com

Democratic gubernatorial candidate Francesca Hong is proposing limits on artificial intelligence in the workplace as part of her emerging technology platform, including prohibiting it from being used to surveil workers while off duty and eliminating tax breaks for companies that use AI to justify layoffs.

Hong, a self-avowed democratic socialist and one of seven Democrats running in a wide-open race to be the state's next top executive, announced the proposal Friday as a complement to her data center platform, which calls for a moratorium on building data centers until environmental and energy impacts are better understood.

Hong's AI proposal would limit "robo bosses" by requiring employers to disclose to employees or prospective employees when automated tools are used to make human resource decisions. It would also ban the technology from monitoring workers outside of work or in non-work areas and make it illegal to use AI to set dynamic wages or make last-minute scheduling decisions.

The proposal would also prohibit layoffs "on the public dime" by barring any companies that get tax credits or contract with the state from using AI technology as a reason to lay off staff.

"Silicon Valley marketers and data center landlords hype AI as the most significant economic transformation since the Industrial Revolution," Hong said in her policy announcement. "Whether that's true or not, CEOs and corporations are acting like it is and we're seeing the consequences in how they treat their workers."

Like many proposed business regulations, such policies would likely face legal challenges assuming they could even pass the Legislature, currently controlled by Republicans.

Special electric rates set for data centers in a first for Wisconsin

Data centers

In a previous gubernatorial candidate forum in October, candidates offered some support for data center construction but all stressed that long-term planning and the state's energy grid's capability to handle such large developments should be considered.

In addition to a temporary ban on new data centers, Hong has called for using renewable energy to power the vast facilities and would prohibit the use of local or state tax breaks in their construction.

U.S. Rep. Tom Tiffany, the Republican frontrunner, said he is against data centers "destroying farmland" and increasing costs for utility ratepayers.

Responses from multiple Marquette Law School state polls in the last year show bipartisan distrust of data centers and artificial intelligence. In an October 2025 poll, about 55% of Wisconsinites polled across the political spectrum said any benefits from data center construction didn't outweigh the risks.

Just four months later, in February, following a handful of high-profile and secretive data center proposals, including one near DeForest that was ultimately defeated, that average disapproval rating for data centers rose to 70%. Republican skepticism was steady but 76% of independents and 85% of Democrats said the costs were greater, citing strains on water and energy resources.

Demand grows

Data centers enormous facilities that require considerable power to store, process and distribute applications, oftentimes artificial intelligence programs were once a rarity in Wisconsin. But in recent years, as usage of the technology by the general public, especially generative AI, has increased, the demand for data servers across the country, and access to power and water to operate and cool them, has intensified.

Wisconsin has at least 50 data centers, according to the industry-tracking website Data Center Map, with several being proposed or in the process of being constructed. The size and cost of those facilities can vary considerably.

State lawmakers attempted to regulate data centers this session but the efforts failed. A Republican-authored bill that would have required data center developers to cover their energy costs sped through the Assembly but stalled in the Senate after it was pulled from the calendar.

Hong is in a packed field of Democratic candidates running for a spot on the November ballot. Other liberals in the race include Lt. Gov. Sara Rodriguez; Milwaukee County Executive David Crowley; former DOA Secretary Joel Brennan; former Wisconsin Economic Development Corp. secretary and CEO Missy Hughes; former Lt. Gov. Mandela Barnes; and state Sen. Kelda Roys, of Madison. Gov. Tony Evers declined to run for a third term.

urn:contentItem:6JHK-F5B3-RRV5-437Y-00000-00CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC - RECENT SECTOR NEWS INCLUDING PEERS2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:40:07ZLexisNexisSTOCKCLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC - RECENT SECTOR NEWS INCLUDING PEERS

DASHBOARD: CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC

PermID4296619307Websitehttp://www.clarkconstruction.comIndustryConstructionAddress18201 Von Karman Avenue Suite 800 Irvine, California 92612

ACTIVITIES:

Clark Construction Group, LLC, founded in 1906, is a leading general contractor headquartered in Bethesda, Maryland. With over 4,000 employees across 20+ offices, it serves commercial, infrastructure, and institutional sectors, delivering projects like stadiums, hospitals, and transit systems. Generating over $5 billion annually, Clark is known for its use of Building Information Modeling (BIM) and sustainable practices. In 2024, it completed major infrastructure projects, emphasizing green construction. Its culture prioritizes safety, diversity, and employee development, earning industry accolades. Competing with Turner Construction, Clark stands out for its large-scale project expertise and client partnerships. Recent initiatives include expanding renewable energy construction and digital tools for project management.

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC [4 Press Releases]

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC GREEN FROM THE INSIDE OUT: BERKELEY LAB SETS NEW STANDARDS

Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory's Biological and Environmental Program Integration Center sets new sustainability standards. Berkeley Lab Biological and Environmental Program Integration Center (BioEPIC) unites scientists from LBNL's biosciences and earth and environmental sciences areas in a four-story, 71,000-square-foot facility that sets a new standard for sustainability. Completed in March, the facility offers a suite of next-generation tools designed to study how microbial communities interact with plants and soil to influence the environment.

Inside, 200 researchers perform carefully controlled experiments on microbe-soil-plant interactions with instruments and computing infrastructure that connect BioEPIC to its field research sites. These unique climate research environments, along with three additional levels of labs and collaborative spaces, are housed in an all-electric facility designed to maximize energy efficiency and resiliency.

By supporting an array of unique research needs in this highly sustainable setting, the BioEPIC

Source: Company Website

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC BUILDERS AT HEART: WINSTON VAN KEULEN

This article is part of our series Builders at Heart, where we highlight the passions and backgrounds of the Clark team - the things that shape us - that allow us to tackle challenges head-on, solve complex problems, and build what matters.

Meet Winston van Keulen, a superintendent with Clark Construction, currently working in San Diego. Discover how he is progressing in his role and the keys to success he shares with new team members.

Source: Company Website

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC FROM CONCEPT TO REALITY: HARNESSING 3D PRINTING AT CLARK'S MID-ATLANTIC YARD

Clark Construction's Mid-Atlantic Yard in Upper Marlboro, Maryland, is a powerhouse of specialized services, from fleet oversight to custom fabrication. This diverse facility is also part of a transformative initiative: the integration of 3D printing for prototyping innovative custom solutions to solve complex challenges.

The method came to life when faced with a challenge: designing a custom cleaning piece for a drill rig auger. While the traditional route would involve costly and time-consuming steel fabrication for a prototype, Charles Harman III, a craft and machine shop supervisor, and his team explored a new solution for this type of fabrication and successfully used a 3D printer to create a scaled model. The successful prototype demonstrated the efficacy of the system, and once the 3D printed model was approved, the team began assembling the steel-fabricated version.

Source: Company Website

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC ENHANCING RICHMOND'S WATER RESILIENCE

Clark Water is currently leading improvements on the Shockoe Diversion Structure in Richmond - a key part of the city's combined sewer overflow (CSO) system, which was originally built in the early 1970s. The project will alleviate sewage and stormwater overflow during high-water events. To achieve this, crews have installed mechanical trash rake screens to prevent debris from passing through, two crest gates to minimize upstream flooding, and are making upgrades to supporting infrastructure.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION [1 Press Release]

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION LABOR SHORTAGE LOOMS AS CONSTRUCTION REBOUNDS

SANTA CLARA, Calif., Dec. 9, 2025 - Just as individual market sectors are finding ways to move projects forward after a year of supply chain and cost turbulence, the ongoing labor shortage threatens to bog down construction unless owners and contractors embrace new approaches, according to the latest Market Conditions Report from DPR Construction, one of the nation's top technical builders.

A recent survey by the Associated General Contractors of America and the national Center for Construction Education and Research showed that:

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION [3 Press Releases]

+ TURNER SELECTED FOR $500 MILLION WESTCOURT ORLANDO MIXED-USE DEVELOPMENT

The 900,000-square-foot project developed by JMA Ventures will create a vibrant destination in downtown Orlando.

Source: Company Website

+ TURNER PROVIDES AI-POWERED SAFETY TOOL TO THE CONSTRUCTION INDUSTRY

Turner is making SafeT Coach, its AI-powered field safety tool, available free of charge to the broader construction industry. The announcement comes during Turner's 22nd annual Safety Week and company-wide Safety Stand-Down, it's largest to date, under the theme "All In Together."

In construction, conditions change frequently, and the traditional safety resources that establish standards-manuals, scheduled training, retrospective reporting-are not always accessible when and where field teams need them most. SafeT Coach was developed to close that gap.

Source: Company Website

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION: BUILDING A SUSTAINABLE FUTURE TOGETHER

Throughout April, including Earth Day on April 22, Turner International Turkiye celebrated Earth Month across all 8 workplaces with a series of inspiring activities focused on sustainability and environmental awareness.

Source: Company Website

value.Title: Wisconsin candidate for governor Francesca Hong proposes limitations on AI in the workplace
value.Overview: Kenosha News: Web Edition Articles (Wisconsin), STATE REGIONAL, 764words

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JH6-BCP3-RWXD-80D9-00000-00Wisconsin candidate for governor Francesca Hong proposes limitations on AI in the workplace2026-05-04T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:40:07ZLexisNexisSTATE REGIONALWisconsin candidate for governor Francesca Hong proposes limitations on AI in the workplaceKIMBERLY WETHAL kwethal@madison.com

Democratic gubernatorial candidate Francesca Hong is proposing limits on artificial intelligence in the workplace as part of her emerging technology platform, including prohibiting it from being used to surveil workers while off duty and eliminating tax breaks for companies that use AI to justify layoffs.

Hong, a self-avowed democratic socialist and one of seven Democrats running in a wide-open race to be the state's next top executive, announced the proposal Friday as a complement to her data center platform, which calls for a moratorium on building data centers until environmental and energy impacts are better understood.

Hong's AI proposal would limit "robo bosses" by requiring employers to disclose to employees or prospective employees when automated tools are used to make human resource decisions. It would also ban the technology from monitoring workers outside of work or in non-work areas and make it illegal to use AI to set dynamic wages or make last-minute scheduling decisions.

The proposal would also prohibit layoffs "on the public dime" by barring any companies that get tax credits or contract with the state from using AI technology as a reason to lay off staff.

"Silicon Valley marketers and data center landlords hype AI as the most significant economic transformation since the Industrial Revolution," Hong said in her policy announcement. "Whether that's true or not, CEOs and corporations are acting like it is and we're seeing the consequences in how they treat their workers."

Like many proposed business regulations, such policies would likely face legal challenges assuming they could even pass the Legislature, currently controlled by Republicans.

Special electric rates set for data centers in a first for Wisconsin

Data centers

In a previous gubernatorial candidate forum in October, candidates offered some support for data center construction but all stressed that long-term planning and the state's energy grid's capability to handle such large developments should be considered.

In addition to a temporary ban on new data centers, Hong has called for using renewable energy to power the vast facilities and would prohibit the use of local or state tax breaks in their construction.

U.S. Rep. Tom Tiffany, the Republican frontrunner, said he is against data centers "destroying farmland" and increasing costs for utility ratepayers.

Responses from multiple Marquette Law School state polls in the last year show bipartisan distrust of data centers and artificial intelligence. In an October 2025 poll, about 55% of Wisconsinites polled across the political spectrum said any benefits from data center construction didn't outweigh the risks.

Just four months later, in February, following a handful of high-profile and secretive data center proposals, including one near DeForest that was ultimately defeated, that average disapproval rating for data centers rose to 70%. Republican skepticism was steady but 76% of independents and 85% of Democrats said the costs were greater, citing strains on water and energy resources.

Demand grows

Data centers enormous facilities that require considerable power to store, process and distribute applications, oftentimes artificial intelligence programs were once a rarity in Wisconsin. But in recent years, as usage of the technology by the general public, especially generative AI, has increased, the demand for data servers across the country, and access to power and water to operate and cool them, has intensified.

Wisconsin has at least 50 data centers, according to the industry-tracking website Data Center Map, with several being proposed or in the process of being constructed. The size and cost of those facilities can vary considerably.

State lawmakers attempted to regulate data centers this session but the efforts failed. A Republican-authored bill that would have required data center developers to cover their energy costs sped through the Assembly but stalled in the Senate after it was pulled from the calendar.

Hong is in a packed field of Democratic candidates running for a spot on the November ballot. Other liberals in the race include Lt. Gov. Sara Rodriguez; Milwaukee County Executive David Crowley; former DOA Secretary Joel Brennan; former Wisconsin Economic Development Corp. secretary and CEO Missy Hughes; former Lt. Gov. Mandela Barnes; and state Sen. Kelda Roys, of Madison. Gov. Tony Evers declined to run for a third term.

Surplus deal, other bills in limbo as Wisconsin legislative session winds down

Wisconsin utilities join effort to curb power line competition

value.Title: Southern Company Could Assess Openings Following Vistra Corporation Bock Moss Landing BESS Litigation on Product Liability Concerns *Updated*

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHD-9093-SDNH-R35M-00000-00Southern Company Could Assess Openings Following Vistra Corporation Bock Moss Landing BESS Litigation on Product Liability Concerns *Updated*2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:40:07ZLexisNexisSouthern Company Could Assess Openings Following Vistra Corporation Bock Moss Landing BESS Litigation on Product Liability Concerns *Updated*

NYSE STOCK REPORT

What Stakeholders Need To Know:

The current legal proceedings present specific implications for organizations managing large-scale battery energy storage within power generation. The litigation highlights operational risks connected to thermal incidents, environmental considerations, and community-level effects stemming from BESS sites. Subsequent legal determinations may alter safety parameters and liability structures concerning storage assets that support grid stability and renewable energy integration. These proceedings might prompt the implementation of stricter industry protocols, notably for installations located near urban centers. Stakeholders are advised to review how these regulatory adjustments affect market positioning, funding strategies, and standard operating procedures within the energy storage sector.

Background of the Case

The Moss Landing BESS fire litigation, which includes Bock v. Vistra et al., No. 5:25-cv-06869 and related actions in the Northern District of California, originates from a January 16, 2025, thermal runaway fire at Vistra's 300 MW lithium-ion battery facility. The incident resulted in temporary evacuations, localized highway closures, and environmental contamination assessments. Plaintiffs have filed claims of negligence, product liability, and nuisance against Vistra subsidiaries alongside LG entities. Motions to dismiss were partially denied in January 2026, and the discovery phase continues. An Environmental Protection Agency settlement outlines specific removal requirements. Vistra subsequently recorded a $400 million write-down and initiated the decommissioning of Moss 100, while eventual remediation costs and damages remain subject to ongoing legal proceedings.

Competitive Impacts on Southern Company

Southern Company's regulated utility framework and existing generation assets may experience a transition toward more methodical storage integration strategies across its designated service regions.

Possible Opportunities for Southern Company

Favorable positioning within regulated storage procurement procedures.

Increased synergies between transmission infrastructure and storage capabilities.

Strengthened client assurance regarding moderated-risk operational frameworks.

Participation in the development of regional safety guidelines.

Why This Case Is Challenging for Southern Company

Indirect compliance requirements and possible ratepayer implications resulting from broader industry cost adjustments.

Key Challenges for Southern Company

Alignment with regulatory bodies regarding safety enhancements.

Evaluation of cost recovery mechanisms.

Management of public communications and community relations.

Prioritization of capital expenditures.

Competitor's Strategic Responses

Implementation of comprehensive internal safety evaluations.

Upgrades to technological and operational monitoring systems.

Expansion of community outreach programs.

Participation in the formulation of industry-wide operational standards.

Key Measures

The deployment of structured governance frameworks, coordinated with regulatory requirements and customer expectations, facilitates orderly implementation.

Possible Losses and Unexpected Advantages

Potential Advantages

Estimated incremental revenues of up to $150 million related to market positioning.

Operational stability within regulated market environments.

Acknowledgment of applied technological methodologies.

Improvement of broader risk management frameworks.

Potential Losses

Estimated operational and compliance costs reaching up to $75 million.

Necessary modifications to existing project timelines and structures.

Effects on overall capital expenditure requirements.

Reallocation of internal organizational resources.

Final Thoughts

Southern Company may utilize its regulated utility framework and emphasis on system reliability to navigate the broader impacts of this litigation, adapting to shifting safety standards in battery storage operations while addressing emerging market conditions.

A year ago the Vistra Corp share price was $139.29. $1,000 would have bought 7.2 Vistra Corp shares on that day. Those 7.2 Shares would be worth $1,152 at today's share price of $160.40. PV$1000= $1,152.

Activities

Vistra Corp is a leading U.S.-based energy company listed on the New York Stock Exchange, specializing in the generation, retail sale, and distribution of electricity across a diverse portfolio of assets. Its core business activities include operating power plants fueled by natural gas, coal, nuclear, and renewables, as well as providing retail energy services to residential, commercial, and industrial customers. The company has strategically expanded into sustainable energy solutions, with its fastest-growing segments being zero-carbon generation and energy storage, particularly through investments in solar and battery technologies. This focus on renewables and innovative retail offerings, such as demand response programs and digital customer platforms, has fueled significant growth amid the transition to cleaner energy sources. Vistra's operations span multiple U.S. regions, emphasizing reliability, efficiency, and environmental stewardship to meet evolving market demands. It is the NYSE's 5th largest Utility company by market capitalisation.

Vistra Corp (NYSE:VST) stands out as a leading entity in the energy sector, known for its robust operations. As a preeminent Vistra energy company, it leverages its diverse portfolio that spans Vistra power generation, Vistra electricity retail, and Vistra energy storage. Integral components include Vistra battery storage projects, Vistra renewable energy, and Vistra solar energy projects, supplemented by Vistra natural gas generation and operations conducted by Luminant (Vistra subsidiary). The firm's presence in Vistra wholesale electricity markets and Vistra retail electricity plans enhances its position. Operational strengths are evident in Vistra ERCOT operations and Vistra demand response programs. The company's strategic focus on sustainability is reflected in its Vistra carbon reduction strategy and Vistra net-zero commitments, detailed in Vistra ESG reporting. Innovations like Vistra digital transformation initiatives and Vistra AI initiatives play a pivotal role, marked by the new Vistra Chief AI and Digital Officer hire. Technological advancements including Vistra predictive maintenance solutions, Vistra grid optimization algorithms, and Vistra smart grid technology optimize resources. Moreover, its prowess in Vistra energy management software, Vistra distributed energy resources is highlighted in the Vistra corporate growth story, supported by up-to-date VST stock news and Vistra investor relations insights.

value.Title: April 28, 2026: San Francisco Breaks Ground on Innovative Fire Training Center as Swinerton Takes CM/GC Role

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHJ-FR23-RTWG-C008-00000-00April 28, 2026: San Francisco Breaks Ground on Innovative Fire Training Center as Swinerton Takes CM/GC Role2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:40:07ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSApril 28, 2026: San Francisco Breaks Ground on Innovative Fire Training Center as Swinerton Takes CM/GC Role

Swinerton Builders has been named Construction Manager/General Contractor for a $145 million San Francisco Fire Department training facility, with San Francisco Public Works as the client. The groundbreaking took place on 16th April 2026 in the city's southeastern district.

The project centers on a 50,000-square-foot scenario district designed to mirror San Francisco neighborhoods, featuring settings such as sloped intersections and tight corridors that simulate post-earthquake conditions.

The facility will also house emergency medical service training, onsite fire apparatus training, and Neighborhood Emergency Response Team sessions, complementing the scenario district.

An Administration/Classroom Building will be designated as an Essential Services Building to allow immediate occupancy after a major earthquake and to serve as a backup emergency operations center; Swinerton highlights its long history of public safety projects in San Francisco.

SECTION 1 SWINERTON INCORPORATED PROFILE

1.1 ACTIVITIES

Swinerton Incorporated provides commercial construction and construction management services in the Western United States. It offers general construction, modernization and renovation, management and consulting, preconstruction, corporate, and renewable energy services. The companys projects include aviation, cultural, education, federal, gaming and entertainment, healthcare, hospitality, interiors, mission critical, mixed-use/residential, office, renewable energy, retail, and special. Swinerton Incorporated was formerly known as Swinerton & Walberg Co. The company was founded in 1888 and is based in San Francisco, California with additional offices in California, Colorado, Hawaii, Texas, New Mexico, and Washington.

value.Title: Vermont Renewable Gas Reaches Agreement With State Agriculture Agency For Lyndon Renewable Energy Project

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHM-0713-S140-50D0-00000-00Vermont Renewable Gas Reaches Agreement With State Agriculture Agency For Lyndon Renewable Energy Project2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:40:07ZLexisNexisVermont Renewable Gas Reaches Agreement With State Agriculture Agency For Lyndon Renewable Energy Project

IRVINE, CA, May 07, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Clean Energy Technologies, Inc. (Nasdaq: CETY) (“CETY” or the“Company”), a clean energy technology and solutions provider focused on converting waste and heat into power and fuels, today announced that its affiliate, Vermont Renewable Gas (VRG), has entered into a Stipulation (MOU) with the Vermont Agency of Agriculture, Food and Markets, establishing comprehensive agricultural and soil protection requirements for its proposed 2.2 MW renewable energy facility in Lyndon. Key Step in the Approval Process
This MOU, filed with the Vermont Public Utility Commission, reflects a full resolution of agricultural impacts and sets detailed conditions governing soil preservation, feedstock sourcing, and biochar quality for the Lyndon project. It represents a significant milestone in the VRG project's review under Vermont's Section 248 process as Vermont Public Utility Commission continues its review. Through compliance with these conditions, the Project is expected to avoid undue adverse impact on the primary agricultural soils. These soils are a critical natural resource that support long-term food production, water retention, ecosystem stability, and sustainable agricultural development.


Protecting Vermont Agricultural Soils
Under the terms of the agreement:

Primary agricultural soils (PAS) impacted by construction will be preserved, managed, and fully restored in accordance with Vermont agricultural standards

Soil structure and integrity must be maintained through pre- and post-construction testing, with strict limits on compaction

Any disturbed soils must be reclaimed and returned to productive agricultural condition, even after project decommissioning

These requirements ensure that agricultural lands remain viable for future use and are protected throughout the life of the project.
Strict Biochar Safety and Environmental Controls
The Project will also be subject to robust testing and operational safeguards, including:

Comprehensive testing of biochar for PFAS, heavy metals, dioxins, PCBs, and other contaminants

Ongoing monitoring of product quality and operating conditions

Prohibition on distribution of biochar if any contaminants exceed regulatory thresholds

Detailed controls on acceptable feedstock, including exclusion of treated wood, plastics, and contaminated materials

The agreement establishes one of the most rigorous frameworks for biochar quality and agricultural safety in the region.
Transparency and Accountability
VRG will be required to:

Maintain traceability of feedstock sources

Notify regulators of any material changes in operations or sourcing

Provide regular testing data and allow regulatory oversight

“These conditions ensure a high standard of accountability,” said Kam Mahdi, CEO.“We are committed to ensuring that this project operates safely, protects Vermont's agricultural resources, and meets the expectations of regulators and the community.”
About Clean Energy Technologies, Inc. (CETY)
Headquartered in Irvine, California, Clean Energy Technologies, Inc. (CETY) is a rising leader in the zero-emission revolution by offering eco-friendly green energy solutions, clean energy fuels and alternative electric power for small and mid-sized projects in North America, Europe, and Asia. CETY also holds a minority ownership interest in, and is affiliated with Vermont renewable Gas LLC. We deliver power from heat and biomass with zero emission and low cost. The Company's principal products are Waste Heat Recovery Solutions using our patented Clean CycleTM generator to create electricity. Waste to Energy Solutions convert waste products created in manufacturing, agriculture, wastewater treatment plants and other industries to electricity and BioChar. Engineering, Consulting and Project Management Solutions provide expertise and experience in developing clean energy projects for municipal and industrial customers and Engineering, Procurement and Construction (EPC) companies.
CETY's common stock is currently traded on the Nasdaq Capital Market under the symbol“CETY.” For more information, visit .
Follow CETY on our social media channels: Twitter | LinkedIn | Facebook
About Vermont Renewable Gas
Vermont Renewable Gas, LLC is a developer of renewable energy infrastructure focused on converting underutilized organic resources into sustainable power solutions. The Company is committed to advancing projects that meet stringent environmental standards while supporting local economies and energy resilience.
This summary should be read in conjunction with the Company's quarterly report on Form 10-Q for the quarterly period ended September 30, 2025 and other periodic filings made pursuant to Section 13 or 15(d) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, which contain, among other matters, risk factors and financial footnotes as well as a discussions of our business, operations and financial matters located on the website of the Securities and Exchange Commission at .

value.Title: CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC - INDUSTRY WATCH: SECTOR TRENDS AND PEER PERSPECTIVES

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHH-MPX3-S7MH-635G-00000-00CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC - INDUSTRY WATCH: SECTOR TRENDS AND PEER PERSPECTIVES2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:40:07ZLexisNexisSTOCKCLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC - INDUSTRY WATCH: SECTOR TRENDS AND PEER PERSPECTIVES

DASHBOARD: CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC

PermID4296619307Websitehttp://www.clarkconstruction.comIndustryConstructionAddress18201 Von Karman Avenue Suite 800 Irvine, California 92612

ACTIVITIES:

Clark Construction Group, LLC, founded in 1906, is a leading general contractor headquartered in Bethesda, Maryland. With over 4,000 employees across 20+ offices, it serves commercial, infrastructure, and institutional sectors, delivering projects like stadiums, hospitals, and transit systems. Generating over $5 billion annually, Clark is known for its use of Building Information Modeling (BIM) and sustainable practices. In 2024, it completed major infrastructure projects, emphasizing green construction. Its culture prioritizes safety, diversity, and employee development, earning industry accolades. Competing with Turner Construction, Clark stands out for its large-scale project expertise and client partnerships. Recent initiatives include expanding renewable energy construction and digital tools for project management.

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC [4 Press Releases]

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC GREEN FROM THE INSIDE OUT: BERKELEY LAB SETS NEW STANDARDS

Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory's Biological and Environmental Program Integration Center sets new sustainability standards. Berkeley Lab Biological and Environmental Program Integration Center (BioEPIC) unites scientists from LBNL's biosciences and earth and environmental sciences areas in a four-story, 71,000-square-foot facility that sets a new standard for sustainability. Completed in March, the facility offers a suite of next-generation tools designed to study how microbial communities interact with plants and soil to influence the environment.

Inside, 200 researchers perform carefully controlled experiments on microbe-soil-plant interactions with instruments and computing infrastructure that connect BioEPIC to its field research sites. These unique climate research environments, along with three additional levels of labs and collaborative spaces, are housed in an all-electric facility designed to maximize energy efficiency and resiliency.

By supporting an array of unique research needs in this highly sustainable setting, the BioEPIC

Source: Company Website

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC BUILDERS AT HEART: WINSTON VAN KEULEN

This article is part of our series Builders at Heart, where we highlight the passions and backgrounds of the Clark team - the things that shape us - that allow us to tackle challenges head-on, solve complex problems, and build what matters.

Meet Winston van Keulen, a superintendent with Clark Construction, currently working in San Diego. Discover how he is progressing in his role and the keys to success he shares with new team members.

Source: Company Website

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC FROM CONCEPT TO REALITY: HARNESSING 3D PRINTING AT CLARK'S MID-ATLANTIC YARD

Clark Construction's Mid-Atlantic Yard in Upper Marlboro, Maryland, is a powerhouse of specialized services, from fleet oversight to custom fabrication. This diverse facility is also part of a transformative initiative: the integration of 3D printing for prototyping innovative custom solutions to solve complex challenges.

The method came to life when faced with a challenge: designing a custom cleaning piece for a drill rig auger. While the traditional route would involve costly and time-consuming steel fabrication for a prototype, Charles Harman III, a craft and machine shop supervisor, and his team explored a new solution for this type of fabrication and successfully used a 3D printer to create a scaled model. The successful prototype demonstrated the efficacy of the system, and once the 3D printed model was approved, the team began assembling the steel-fabricated version.

Source: Company Website

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC ENHANCING RICHMOND'S WATER RESILIENCE

Clark Water is currently leading improvements on the Shockoe Diversion Structure in Richmond - a key part of the city's combined sewer overflow (CSO) system, which was originally built in the early 1970s. The project will alleviate sewage and stormwater overflow during high-water events. To achieve this, crews have installed mechanical trash rake screens to prevent debris from passing through, two crest gates to minimize upstream flooding, and are making upgrades to supporting infrastructure.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION [1 Press Release]

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION LABOR SHORTAGE LOOMS AS CONSTRUCTION REBOUNDS

SANTA CLARA, Calif., Dec. 9, 2025 - Just as individual market sectors are finding ways to move projects forward after a year of supply chain and cost turbulence, the ongoing labor shortage threatens to bog down construction unless owners and contractors embrace new approaches, according to the latest Market Conditions Report from DPR Construction, one of the nation's top technical builders.

A recent survey by the Associated General Contractors of America and the national Center for Construction Education and Research showed that:

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION [3 Press Releases]

+ TURNER SELECTED FOR $500 MILLION WESTCOURT ORLANDO MIXED-USE DEVELOPMENT

The 900,000-square-foot project developed by JMA Ventures will create a vibrant destination in downtown Orlando.

Source: Company Website

+ TURNER PROVIDES AI-POWERED SAFETY TOOL TO THE CONSTRUCTION INDUSTRY

Turner is making SafeT Coach, its AI-powered field safety tool, available free of charge to the broader construction industry. The announcement comes during Turner's 22nd annual Safety Week and company-wide Safety Stand-Down, it's largest to date, under the theme "All In Together."

In construction, conditions change frequently, and the traditional safety resources that establish standards-manuals, scheduled training, retrospective reporting-are not always accessible when and where field teams need them most. SafeT Coach was developed to close that gap.

Source: Company Website

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION: BUILDING A SUSTAINABLE FUTURE TOGETHER

Throughout April, including Earth Day on April 22, Turner International Turkiye celebrated Earth Month across all 8 workplaces with a series of inspiring activities focused on sustainability and environmental awareness.

Source: Company Website

SECTION 2 CORPORATE PROFILE

2.1 Activities

The Nuveen California AMT-Free Quality Municipal Income Fund is a closed-end investment company listed on the NYSE, focusing on managing a diversified portfolio of high-quality municipal bonds issued within California. Its core business activities center on investing in tax-exempt securities that provide income free from federal and, for California residents, state taxes, thereby appealing to investors seeking stable, low-risk returns. The fund emphasizes sectors such as education, healthcare, and infrastructure, which form the foundation of its strategy to support public projects while generating reliable dividends. Among its fastest-growing segments are bonds tied to sustainable initiatives, including renewable energy and water infrastructure, fueled by California's aggressive environmental policies and population growth. This focus has driven increased demand, enhancing the fund's asset base and positioning it to capitalize on the state's economic expansion through prudent risk management and long-term yield optimization.
