value.Title: California investigates Trump administration's deal to end an offshore wind project

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JH9-GYY3-S9GF-H52B-00000-00California investigates Trump administration's deal to end an offshore wind project2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:40:07ZLexisNexisENVIRONMENTCalifornia investigates Trump administration's deal to end an offshore wind projectJENNIFER McDERMOTT

California is investigating one of the Trump administration's deals to end an offshore wind project.

Golden State Wind was a floating offshore wind project proposed off California's central coast. The California Energy Commission said Monday it issued an administrative subpoena to Golden State Wind.

The commission said it is seeking documents and information about the company's recent agreement with the Department of Interior to accept a payout in exchange for voluntarily abandoning its offshore wind lease.

"The Trump administration is recklessly spending billions of taxpayer dollars on backroom deals that would turn back the clock on innovation," CEC Chair David Hochschild said in a statement. "Californians deserve immediate answers about the nature of this payout. Taxpayer dollars should be used to build a sustainable energy future, not to pay to make projects disappear."

The Trump administration is spending nearly $2 billion to get energy companies to walk away from U.S. offshore wind projects. Interior Secretary Doug Burgum said companies were sold a product that was only viable when propped up by massive taxpayer subsidies when they bid for these offshore wind leases in 2022, under former President Joe Biden.

The Republican administration adopted this strategy after federal courts thwarted President Donald Trump's efforts to stop offshore wind development through executive action. Three agreements have been announced.

Under the first deal, made public in March, French company TotalEnergies is getting $1 billion essentially a refund of its leases for offshore wind projects off North Carolina and New York if it invests the money in fossil fuel projects instead. In the latest deals announced last week, the administration said Golden State Wind and Bluepoint Wind agreed to end their leases in exchange for reimbursements totaling nearly $900 million, provided they invest equally in fossil fuels.

Both Golden State and Bluepoint are co-owned by Ocean Winds, a joint venture of EDP Renewables and French energy giant Engie. Bluepoint Wind was an offshore wind project in the early stages of development off the coasts of New Jersey and New York.

When asked about the subpoena Monday, Ocean Winds said it does not comment on open or potential litigation.

This investigation sets the stage for legal action from California to safeguard renewable energy, as well as the thousands of jobs and millions of dollars of investment the state was counting on, said Eddie Ahn, executive director of Brightline Defense, an environmental justice nonprofit working to advance offshore wind in California.

A letter from California Attorney General Rob Bonta's office to Golden State Wind says the state anticipates potential litigation involving the federal government and parties to lease buyouts impacting California's energy needs and offshore wind programs. California has invested about $100 million to support offshore wind development in order to accelerate the state's transition to clean energy and address climate change.

Democrats in Congress are investigating, too. U.S. Reps. Jared Huffman of California, the top Democrat on the House Natural Resources Committee, and Jamie Raskin, the ranking Democrat on the House Judiciary Committee, are demanding information about TotalEnergies agreement.

Media Release

State Grid Corporation of China - State Grid Corporation of China's achievements in digital and intelligent transformation were showcased at the Digital China Construction Summit. - 4/5/2026

On April 29, the 9th Digital China Summit opened in Fuzhou, Fujian Province. The State Grid Corporation of China's experience zone made a splendid appearance under the theme "Let Light Meet Transparency," showcasing its latest achievements, including an embodied intelligent robot and several first-of-its-kind major technological equipment, demonstrating the company's latest progress in digital grids, intelligent equipment, core technologies, and provincial-level demonstrations.

The State Grid Experience Zone is divided into five sections: Core Achievements Area, Smart State Grid Supporting Digital China Achievements Area, Three Thematic Exhibition Areas, Digital Fujian Power Supporting Digital Fujian Achievements Area, and Interactive Experience Area. The central area of the exhibition hall showcases a four-dimensional intelligent robot army: "Sky Eyes, Ground Guardians, Underground Vanguards, and Special Forces," systematically presenting the applications of various intelligent robots in different professional fields. The Core Achievements Exhibition Area highlights the domestically developed large-scale power system electromagnetic transient simulation platform. This platform boasts over 100,000 nodes, a 4-minute ultra-fast simulation capability, and a 97% renewable energy absorption rate, ensuring the State Grid's over 1.4 billion kilowatts of renewable energy installed capacity and 370 million kilowatts of inter-regional and inter-provincial power transmission capacity. The Thematic Exhibition Area presents 26 typical digital and intelligent scenarios across three themes: "Fulfilling National Strategic Missions, Ensuring National Energy Security, and Serving the National Economy and People's Livelihood," demonstrating State Grid's focus on independent control of key core technologies and leading the accelerated upgrading of energy and power equipment towards high-end, intelligent, and domestically produced technologies.

In addition, the "Digital Fujian Power Empowering Digital Fujian" exhibition area focuses on core scenarios such as the new power system dispatch digital "cockpit," intelligent distribution network diagnosis and analysis, and intelligent electricity billing services, comprehensively showcasing the innovative practices of State Grid Fujian Power's deep integration of digital technology and power grid business. The interactive exhibition area features an interactive experience zone called "A Day in the Life of a Power Grid Worker," which uses virtual reality and AI technology to allow ordinary visitors to experience four core business scenarios—transmission line inspection, substation operation, distribution maintenance, and power supply and consumption services—in an immersive way.

This year marks the sixth consecutive year that the State Grid Corporation of China has been invited to host the Smart Energy Sub-Forum at the Digital China Summit. The forum, themed "Building a New Ecosystem Empowered by Digital Intelligence and Jointly Drawing a New Blueprint for Future Energy," invited eight industry experts, including academicians from the Chinese Academy of Engineering, professors from research institutes, senior executives from energy companies, and leading figures from internet companies, to attend and deliver keynote speeches. Representatives from government agencies such as the State-owned Assets Supervision and Administration Commission of the State Council, the National Energy Administration, the National Data Bureau, and the Fujian Provincial Development and Reform Commission, as well as over 120 energy and power companies, industry associations, internet companies, and research institutes, participated. The forum aimed to continuously build efficient communication bridges, facilitate extensive exchanges and deepen cooperation among all parties, build consensus, share opportunities for the integrated development of digital intelligence, jointly build a new future for smart energy development, and contribute the wisdom and strength of the energy industry to the in-depth promotion of Digital China construction and the achievement of the "dual carbon" target.

The company will leverage digitalization and intelligentization as its driving force to accelerate the construction of a world-leading energy internet enterprise with Chinese characteristics, continuously launch more new technologies, achievements, and products, and contribute more to the construction of Digital China and high-quality economic and social development with higher-level technological innovation and better energy services.

State Grid Corporation of China - On duty during the holidays | Ensuring worry-free green travel - 2/5/2026

Tourist attractions, family visits, and road trips... the May Day holiday saw a surge in travel across the country, leading to a boom in demand for charging services for new energy vehicles. Data from the State Grid Smart Vehicle Network Platform shows that on May 1st, the platform's charging volume reached 35.8053 million kilowatt-hours, a year-on-year increase of 21.86%; charging orders totaled 1.4205 million, a year-on-year increase of 20.51%; and highway charging volume reached 11.274 million kilowatt-hours, a year-on-year increase of 23.29%, all setting new historical records.

State Grid Corporation of China has proactively planned and meticulously arranged for the continuous improvement of charging infrastructure layout, enhanced data monitoring and forecasting, and improved operation and maintenance services to meet the charging needs of new energy vehicles during the holidays. Various units within the company have focused on key charging stations along highways, near scenic areas, and in commercial hubs, organizing employees to conduct special inspections and maintenance, and ensuring adequate staffing for green travel during the holidays.

Technological upgrades meet the demand for high-speed charging

"Hello, do you need to charge your car?" On May 1st, at the Xin'an service area on the G5 Beijing-Kunming Expressway, an employee of the State Grid Sichuan Mianyang Power Supply Company asked Mr. Zhao, a new energy vehicle owner, who was waiting in line to charge his car. As they spoke, a white mobile charging robot slowly drove over and stopped next to Mr. Zhao's vehicle. Guided by the power supply employee, Mr. Zhao completed the QR code scanning process, and the charging robot extended its robotic arm to precisely connect to the charging port. The car's LCD screen then displayed that charging had begun.

"I was worried that there wouldn't be enough charging spaces on the road, but I didn't expect that the mobile charging robot here can drive right next to the car to charge. It's so convenient!" Mr. Zhao said.

In response to the surge in travel and charging demand during the May Day holiday, State Grid Sichuan Electric Power proactively connected with transportation departments to share real-time monitoring data on highway traffic flow and charging load. This allowed for accurate prediction of peak charging times, and the deployment of mobile charging robots in service areas with high charging demand. These robots provide dynamic charging services between parking spaces, achieving an upgrade from "cars looking for charging stations" to "charging stations chasing cars."

Employees of the State Grid Shandong Heze Power Supply Company inspect charging facilities at highway service areas.

Mobile charging technology is constantly iterating and upgrading, providing new energy vehicles with more stable "mobile charging stations." At the Xinqiao service area on the Shanghai-Shaanxi Expressway, the State Grid Anhui Hefei Power Supply Company deployed a mobile emergency energy storage and charging vehicle integrating energy storage, charging, and photovoltaic power replenishment. This vehicle is equipped with a 500 kW/500 kWh onboard energy storage system and two 120 kW DC bidirectional charging piles. It can quickly replenish the energy of new energy vehicles and store excess electricity back into the energy storage system. In the event of an external power outage, it can also rely on the photovoltaic panels on its roof for supplemental power. During the May Day holiday, this mobile emergency energy storage and charging vehicle was deployed to busy locations such as highway charging stations and urban fast-charging stations according to changes in charging load, providing backup power to charging stations and alleviating the pressure of charging queues during peak holiday periods.

Employees of the State Grid Anhui Hefei Power Supply Company guide drivers to use mobile power supply vehicles for charging at the Fengle Service Area on the G3 Beijing-Taipei Expressway.

For owners of new energy vehicles traveling long distances, the speed of charging and the length of queues directly impact their travel experience. On May 1st, at the integrated photovoltaic-storage-charging smart supercharging station in the Tonglu Service Area (Qiandao Lake direction) of the G25 Changshen Expressway, Shen Tuchengfei, an employee of the State Grid Zhejiang Hangzhou Tonglu County Power Supply Company, conducted a special holiday inspection of charging stations while wearing AI smart inspection glasses. This station and its "sister" supercharging station (Hangzhou direction) are currently the largest highway supercharging stations in China, with 216 charging spaces in both directions, capable of meeting the charging needs of up to 6,000 vehicles per day.

“During the May Day holiday, our inspection mode changed from fixed-sequence inspection to mobile inspection, responding to various charging needs of car owners at any time. The 78 liquid-cooled supercharging piles with a maximum single-gun power of 600 kilowatts are equipped in the station, which can achieve ultra-fast charging of 'one kilometer per second', allowing new energy vehicle owners to enjoy the convenient experience of 'charging as soon as you arrive and leaving as soon as you charge',” said Shen Tuchengfei.

Proactive services enhance scenic area operation and maintenance support

On May 2nd, the G331 highway, known as the "Most Beautiful National Highway" in Tonghua, Jilin Province, saw a peak in passenger traffic. Employees from the State Grid Tonghua Power Supply Company visited the Ruliuyuan RV Campground in Ji'an to assist in inspecting charging stations, wiring, and other equipment for potential hazards. "With the increasing number of new energy vehicles, reliable charging is crucial for enhancing the campground's visitor experience," said Yin Longfei, the campground's manager.

Employees of the State Grid Jilin Tonghua Power Supply Company promote safe electricity use knowledge in scenic areas along the G331 highway.

In response to the high-traffic areas such as scenic spots, commercial districts, and highway hubs, State Grid Jilin Electric Power optimized its emergency plan for charging and battery swapping services during the May Day holiday, implemented a 24-hour duty system, and organized employees to proactively provide convenient services such as charging guidance. At the same time, the company conducted a comprehensive inspection of its 127 self-owned charging and battery swapping stations, strengthened the operation and maintenance of key stations and online monitoring, and enhanced power supply monitoring of third-party charging facilities to fully ensure the safe, green, and smooth travel of new energy vehicles.

Shanxi Province boasts numerous scenic spots and historical sites, and the May Day holiday saw a surge in self-driving tourists, significantly increasing the pressure on charging stations around these areas. State Grid Shanxi Electric Power Company initiated its holiday charging station maintenance work ahead of schedule, conducting comprehensive safety inspections of 113 highway service areas and 35 scenic area charging stations to ensure a stable charging pile availability rate of over 99.5%. During the May Day holiday, the company continuously strengthened charging station operation and maintenance, utilizing AI-powered intelligent cloud monitoring technology to monitor station conditions in real time online, and organizing emergency repair teams to be on-site at key maintenance stations throughout the province. Mobile energy storage vehicles, mobile generators, and emergency charging cabins were deployed in service areas with high passenger traffic, such as Mengxian, Pingyao, and Wutaishan, to flexibly alleviate charging pressure during peak periods.

Employees of the State Grid Sichuan Guangyuan Power Supply Company conducted an inspection of charging facilities at the Cangxi Pear Expo Park.

In the Grand Canyon Scenic Area of Nanfen District, Benxi, Liaoning Province, the rolling green hills are covered in new life, and a steady stream of new energy self-driving vehicles are coming to enjoy the spring scenery. Parking spaces equipped with charging stations are particularly sought after in the scenic area's parking lot. To cope with the peak charging season during the May Day holiday, the State Grid Benxi Power Supply Company organized its employees to conduct special safety inspections of charging stations at various scenic spots to ensure that every charging station is in good working order. They also proactively liaised with the scenic area operators to optimize charging guidance services and improve the charging experience for tourists.

Employees of the State Grid Fujian Sanming Shaxian District Power Supply Company conducted a special inspection of new energy vehicle charging stations.

During the May Day holiday, cultural and tourism activities such as "Seaside Fun" and "Slow Wave Lifestyle Festival" held in Shishi City, Quanzhou, Fujian Province, attracted a large number of tourists. The State Grid Quanzhou Shishi Power Supply Company strengthened inspections of charging stations and homestay charging piles in popular scenic spots such as Hongta Bay and Golden Coast, and enhanced emergency power supply and maintenance during the holiday, ensuring "charging piles are in the scenic area, service is with you," allowing tourists from all over to enjoy the mountains and sea of Shishi.

“Scenic spots and guesthouses all have charging stations, so we no longer have travel anxiety when we go out. It’s becoming more and more convenient to travel in new energy vehicles!” said Ms. Wang, a new energy vehicle owner who drove to Shishi City for a trip.

Optimizing the layout will help promote green and smooth travel in urban and rural areas.

"I didn't expect the charging stations in town to charge so quickly. It was fully charged in the time it took me to go out for a meal!" On May 1st, Mr. Zhang, who was traveling from Jilin to Inner Mongolia, charged his car at the charging station in the Ecological Park Service Area of Daqintala Town, Naiman Banner, Tongliao. The newly put into operation 160-kilowatt dual-gun fast charging station can quickly replenish vehicles' power in a short time, allowing tourists and villagers returning to their hometowns to enjoy "stop and charge instantly, seamless charging" services in the town.

Employees of the State Grid Inner Mongolia East Tongliao Naiman Banner Power Supply Company inspect the operation of charging stations in the scenic area.

“During the May Day holiday, we deployed emergency repair teams to key areas on 24/7, and relied on the intelligent vehicle network platform to monitor the operational status of charging facilities in towns and villages in real time, achieving 24-hour remote monitoring and second-level fault response, further optimizing the charging experience for customers,” said Zhang Liang, deputy director of the Marketing Department of State Grid Tongliao Power Supply Company in Inner Mongolia East. The company has established a “20-minute emergency service circle” for green travel in urban and rural areas, increased the deployment of mobile charging equipment, improved peak service capacity, and ensured that repair personnel can quickly arrive at the scene, fully guaranteeing safe and smooth travel for owners of new energy vehicles.

With the continued popularity of short-distance and local travel, the demand for charging in areas surrounding cities is rapidly increasing, and supporting charging facilities are also being upgraded. On May 1st, Mr. Li from Jiangbei New Area in Nanjing, Jiangsu Province, visited the Gaocun RV Base in Yaxi International Slow City, Gaochun District, and used the upgraded DC fast charging pile to fully charge his car in just 30 minutes. Before the holiday, the State Grid Nanjing Gaochun District Power Supply Company upgraded the rated power of the six DC fast charging piles at the site from 60 kilowatts to 80 kilowatts, increasing the charging speed by 33%.

"I never expected to find charging stations in the mountains, especially those using solar-powered electricity." On the same day, at the charging station in the village square of Habancha Village, Yuzhong County, Lanzhou, Gansu Province, Mr. Wang, a car owner who drove his new energy vehicle from downtown Lanzhou to take his family for a spring outing, praised the village's solar-powered charging station. The roof of this solar-powered charging station is covered with solar photovoltaic panels, and the green electricity generated directly supplies four DC charging piles below, realizing "self-generation and self-consumption, local consumption," which not only protects the local natural ecology but also extends green travel to the "last mile."

Employees of the State Grid Zhejiang Lishui Qingyuan County Power Supply Company conduct a special inspection of charging stations at the Qingyuan County Passenger Transport Center.

The urban and rural charging network is becoming increasingly dense, and services are extending to more dimensions. At the Longdu Town charging station in Zhucheng City, Weifang, Shandong Province, Mr. Liu from Tai'an took a break from charging at the "Rainbow Station." The grid manager of the Longdu Power Supply Station of the State Grid Weifang Zhucheng Power Supply Company, who was on duty, warmly introduced him to the surrounding attractions. For charging stations near suburban tourist attractions and along tourist routes, State Grid Shandong Electric Power fully leverages the advantage of its grid managers' familiarity with local conditions, proactively providing extended services. While offering charging guidance to car owners, they also act as local tourism "promoters," making green travel in urban and rural areas more accessible and contributing to the development of the local cultural and tourism economy.

State Grid Corporation of China - During the May Day holiday, the State Grid operated safely and provided reliable power supply; labor illuminated the light, and craftsmanship safeguarded warmth. - 1/5/2026

During the May Day holiday, with spring in full bloom, China experienced a peak in travel and consumption. While people enjoyed the beautiful spring weather, the staff of the State Grid Corporation of China remained on the front lines of power maintenance and protection, ensuring the safe and stable operation of the power grid and a reliable power supply. During the holiday, the State Grid Corporation coordinated the deployment of emergency teams, repair vehicles, and power generation equipment, focusing on key locations such as transportation hubs, tourist attractions, and highway service areas to conduct power supply inspections, promptly identifying and eliminating potential equipment hazards, and safeguarding the lights of countless homes with a robust power grid.

Lingshao ±800 kV UHVDC transmission project (Zhejiang section)

Key projects are being constructed without interruption, prioritizing timely completion.

Springtime is in full bloom, and now is the perfect time to forge ahead. The May Day holiday is the peak season for power grid construction, and State Grid employees are staying on the front lines to accelerate the construction of key projects.

In Zhangjiakou, Hebei Province, the construction site of the Datong-Huailai-Tianjin South 1000 kV ultra-high voltage AC power transmission project is bustling with activity. Nearly 700 construction workers are on duty, with all six core work areas in full swing. State Grid Hebei Electric Power has established an integrated "air-ground-space" intelligent monitoring system covering the entire site. Satellite remote sensing, drones, and ground video signals are transmitted back to the control center, where an artificial intelligence system automatically analyzes and assesses the data, improving work efficiency by more than 50%. Upon completion, the North China 1000 kV main grid will be further improved, promoting the consumption of over 14.6 billion kilowatt-hours of renewable energy annually.

State Grid Chongqing Electric Power employees are installing power lines at the Banan Xinyu 500 kV transmission and transformation project.

In Aba Prefecture, Sichuan Province, on the first phase of the Aba-Chengdu East 1000 kV UHV AC transmission project, tower components are being precisely hoisted into place. May is a precious window of clear weather on the western Sichuan plateau. Nearly 5,000 construction workers remain on site, utilizing innovative methods such as mountain-climbing transport vehicles and a 3D precision construction planning system to overcome challenges such as high altitude and rugged terrain, ensuring the towers are steadily erected and grown amidst the mountains. The project is scheduled to be completed and put into operation by the end of 2026, delivering clean energy from western Sichuan to the load center of the Chengdu metropolitan area.

In Datong, Shanxi Province, thousands of construction workers are on duty at the construction site of the supporting power grid project for the Jinbei New Energy Base, accelerating the construction of three ultra-high voltage (UHV) projects and four 500 kV projects. Inside the 1000 kV Datong UHV substation, six giant transformers weighing over 300 tons each are being installed. Zhou Zichao, head of the Second Project Department of the State Grid Shanxi Electric Power Construction Branch, explained that more than 4,800 people remained at various work sites across the seven projects during the holiday period, working tirelessly to advance the construction.

Employees of the State Grid Shanxi Power Transmission and Transformation Company are working on tower erection in Section 4 of the Yantai-Weihai 1000 kV UHV project.

In Anhui Province, at the construction site of the Gansu-Zhejiang ±800 kV ultra-high voltage direct current (UHVDC) transmission project (Anhui Section 3), power construction workers are carrying out conductor and ground wire erection, tower assembly, and Yangtze River crossing section foundation pouring work in an orderly manner. As the first full-capacity UHVDC flexible DC project in China, the project will strongly promote the transmission of clean energy from the vast western desert and Gobi desert regions after its commissioning. Projects such as the ±800 kV Hezhou converter station and the Anhui-Hubei back-to-back interconnection Susong converter station are also being accelerated, providing strong support for improving the regional power supply security capacity.

In Fujian, the Fuzhou Guanshan 220 kV transmission and transformation project has entered its final construction phase. As the first grid connection hub for a batch of 18 MW offshore wind farms in China, the project will be able to transmit over 3 billion kWh of wind power annually upon completion, equivalent to reducing carbon emissions by 1.338 million tons. During the holiday, 20 power grid construction projects in Fuzhou were progressing simultaneously, with over 300 construction workers remaining on the front lines, accelerating the power grid construction.

Precise measures ensure smooth and green travel during the holidays

During the May Day holiday, the public's demand for spring outings and travel was concentrated, leading to a significant increase in the demand for charging new energy vehicles. The State Grid Corporation made advance arrangements and meticulous plans to ensure that new energy vehicle owners could "depart with a full charge and return with peace of mind."

To cope with peak charging times, State Grid Smart Vehicle Networking Technology Co., Ltd. has selected 3,992 key support sites and formulated a support plan for highway charging stations according to the principle of "one plan for each site". Mobile charging equipment is dynamically deployed according to changes in road network traffic, and on-site duty personnel are arranged to actively respond to peak charging times.

Employees of the State Grid Anhui Tongling Power Supply Company conducted a special inspection of charging piles during the May Day holiday.

In Beijing, State Grid Beijing Electric Power has ensured the charging facilities at 22 highway service areas, 5 transportation hubs, and 50 tourist attractions. Six temporary charging piles have been added at the Baige Service Area on the Beijing-Tibet Expressway, the Taishitun Service Area on the Daqing-Guangzhou Expressway, and the Doudian Service Area on the Beijing-Hong Kong-Macau Expressway. The company has coordinated the deployment of 3,333 emergency repair personnel for the main and distribution networks, building a comprehensive emergency support system and utilizing the "Power Grid Map" system to achieve automatic fault warning, precise location, and one-click dispatch.

In Shandong, State Grid Shandong Electric Power achieved full coverage of public charging stations in all townships across the province by October 2024, and a total of 4,572 charging stations had been built and put into operation by the end of March this year. During the holidays, the company relied on the "remote monitoring + intelligent operation and maintenance + on-site duty" model, giving full play to the advantages of the grid managers of the grassroots power supply stations who were familiar with the local conditions, and provided extended services. In the Taishan Scenic Area, Shandong Taian Power Supply Company relied on the "5G + Smart Taishan" power supply service platform to monitor the entire process of transmission, transformation, distribution, and consumption in real time, and used digital twin technology to create detailed models of important equipment to ensure reliable power supply to the scenic area.

Employees of the State Grid Tianjin Jizhou Power Supply Company inspect charging facilities at a charging station in the Panshan Scenic Area.

Green electricity supply ensures a robust energy transmission artery.

In Inner Mongolia, the Mengxi-Beijing-Tianjin-Hebei ±800 kV ultra-high-voltage direct current (UHVDC) project is progressing rapidly. As Inner Mongolia's first power transmission channel for the Kubuqi Desert's renewable energy base, this project carries the important mission of transmitting 12 million kilowatts of renewable energy power from the Kubuqi Desert to the load center in North China. During the holiday, two construction teams from the Mengxi 3 section simultaneously carried out foundation pouring work. After completion, the project will be able to transmit over 36 billion kilowatt-hours of clean electricity to North China annually. In addition, the Chifeng 1 million kilowatt desert wind-solar-storage base power transmission and transformation project is also under construction. State Grid Inner Mongolia East Electric Power employees hoist the framework of the newly built Burtao substation, part of the Ordos 4 million kilowatt new energy transmission project at the Kubuqi Desert base .

In Hebei Province, construction is in full swing at the Xiongnan converter station of the Mengxi-Beijing-Tianjin-Hebei ±800 kV ultra-high voltage direct current transmission project. With the successful completion of more than 12,000 pile foundations, the project has entered the large-scale foundation construction phase. During the holiday, three civil engineering construction companies and more than 500 construction workers carried out work such as reinforcing steel binding and concrete pouring for electrical equipment foundations in an orderly manner. After completion, the project will be able to transmit more than 36 billion kilowatt-hours of clean electricity to the load center of Hebei Province annually, forming a key "artery" for the region's high-proportion renewable energy consumption.

During the holiday period, State Grid Xinjiang Electric Power deepened its "three-dimensional inspection + centralized monitoring" model, comprehensively utilizing technologies such as drones and online monitoring to conduct special inspections at key stations such as the ±800 kV Barkol converter station. They closely monitored weather warnings, accurately assessed equipment operating status, and comprehensively strengthened the power grid's safety defenses. Currently, Xinjiang's installed capacity of new energy has reached 173 million kilowatts, accounting for over 60% of the total. Relying on the "seven-ring internal supply network and five external transmission channels" main grid, the power transmission capacity reaches 33 million kilowatts, covering 23 provinces, autonomous regions, and municipalities across the country, continuously providing strong support for national energy supply and green transformation.

State Grid Heilongjiang Electric Power employees erect iron towers at the site of the 2 million kilowatt wind power transmission project in Tonghe, Harbin.

Latest Research

Assessing the impact of weather uncertainty on power-load uncertainty and balancing characteristic in buildings with semi-transparent photovoltaic windows

Jinwei Li, Xiaoling Cao, Peixian Zuo, Yanping Yuan, Shijie Jiang, Bo Gao

Abstract

Semi-transparent photovoltaic (PV) windows can simultaneously generate electricity and significantly reshape building cooling and heating loads. Although their energy performance has been widely investigated, the influence of weather uncertainty on the coupled power–load behavior of PV-window-integrated buildings remains poorly understood, and dedicated quantitative indicators for characterizing such coupling are still lacking. Against the backdrop of climate warming and increasingly complex, non-stationary meteorological conditions, understanding weather-uncertainty-induced source–load interactions is essential for the robust design and operation of building energy systems. To bridge this gap, this study develops a weather-uncertainty modeling framework and proposes two novel indicators to quantitatively evaluate power–load uncertainty and balance in buildings equipped with semi-transparent PV windows. Furthermore, the proposed approach is applied to multiple representative climate zones in China to systematically analyze power–load uncertainty characteristics and matching performance under diverse climatic and solar conditions. Results show that the weather uncertainty method proposed in this study is effective. The semi-transparent PV windows can reduce cooling load uncertainty by 2.46% - 61.73% and the magnitude by 12.05%- 75.06%. However, heating load uncertainty and magnitude may increase by 3.25% - 21.09% and 9.04% - 13.72%, respectively. Additionally, the probability that semi-transparent windows have a positive impact on the power-load balance ranges from 25.0% - 62.5%, primarily by reducing total building loads and increasing PV output. These findings contribute quantitative evidence and methodological insights to the uncertainty-aware evaluation and application of semi-transparent photovoltaic windows.

The Industry

13/1/2026

2026 Renewable Energy Outlook: A Potentially Quieter Year, But Steady Growth to Continue

While most experts anticipate a potentially quieter 2026 for the renewable energy industry, new capacity deployment is expected to continue growing, primarily driven by China. This will ultimately seal the fate of coal power, further accelerating its decline. Still, more ambitious and urgent action is needed to achieve a triple increase in renewable energy capacity by 2030.

Despite 2025 seeing unprecedented rollbacks in climate commitments, unambitious NDC submissions and a COP30 that failed to deliver tangible outcomes for global climate action, it proved to be yet another year of record growth in renewables capacity deployment, and experts remain hopeful that the outlook for 2026 will continue this trend, although at a slower pace. As in recent years, China, in particular, will lead the global surge, filling a vacuum left on the global clean energy stage by President Donald Trump’s administration in the US. This will open up immense opportunities for ASEAN nations to advance their climate goals and equip their economies with cleaner, more reliable and more affordable energy.

The 2026 Outlook For Renewable Energy: Renewables Now the World’s Top Power Source

In July 2025, the International Energy Agency (IEA) projected that renewable energy would overtake coal to become the world’s top source of electricity by “2026 at the latest”. The forecast proved accurate as solar and wind met and exceeded all demand growth in the first half of 2025, leading to renewables overtaking coal for the first time and fossil generation falling slightly.

According to the IEA, in 2026, renewables will make up 36% of global power supplies, against just 32% from coal, the fuel’s lowest share in a century. Furthermore, the global share of electricity generation from wind and solar will rise to nearly 20%.

In total, the rapid solar expansion is on course to push the PV market value toward USD 333.7 billion by 2026.

However, S&P Global expects solar to reach a temporary peak in 2026, marking the first annual slowdown in renewables additions after robust growth at the end of 2025, albeit by less than 10%. Still, the experts note that total installed capacity will continue to double through 2030, with emerging markets playing a significant role.

The analysts also see grid infrastructure modernisation and buildup moving into centre stage as a way to improve energy security and reliability throughout 2026. This segment, which has continuously lagged the pace needed to keep up with clean energy deployment across most markets, has often proved the energy transition’s weakest link, creating significant bottlenecks.

Energy Transition in Asia

In 2026, Asia will continue to drive the global clean energy transition, focusing on scaling up renewable energy capacity and increasingly reducing reliance on traditional sources. According to the Economist Intelligence Unit, the current year will mark the beginning of a long-term decline for coal, while renewables will expand at scale, led by China.

For example, Malaysia is one of the countries in the region that will actively pursue this path, with power demand to rise significantly in 2026 and 2027, driven by sectors such as data centres and high-demand industries. India’s energy demand outlook is also on a growth trajectory, with forecasts pointing to an up to 6.5% CAGR from FY 2026-2030.

However, others, like Japan, for example, are planning to push harder for alternative power sources, such as nuclear power, and aim at derailing recent progress in solar power in the country, including by cutting subsidies for large-scale projects. The moves are a part of the ruling party’s efforts to reduce the role of renewables in the energy mix. Considering Japan’s strong influence in the design of the energy system plans of many Asian nations and the increased push for adopting LNG and fossil fuel-based technologies, there are concerns that its fossil fuel lobby will push even harder to undermine the region’s energy transition and climate goals, especially when renewables are proving the more economically sound and secure choice.

As a result, Japan’s push has faced growing opposition across the region. In a joint statement from October 2025, 36 leading Asian civil society organisations warned that Japan’s technologies under the Asia Zero Emission Community (AZEC) won’t cut emissions at the scale or speed required, but will instead lock countries into long-term fossil fuel use, expensive infrastructure and debt, while delaying the renewable energy transition.

“Japan has the resources and the responsibility to support Asia’s renewable energy transition, and that begins with PM Takaichi’s political will,” said Makiko Arima from Oil Change International.

China to Remain in the Spotlight in and Beyond 2026

S&P Global warns that, in 2026, China’s annual additions will fall to 200 GW from approximately 300 GW in 2025, a decline so steep that no other region will be able to compensate. This is expected to be the result of a policy shift from guaranteed pricing to competitive bidding, which took place in mid-2025 and triggered a dramatic slowdown after an initial rush of installations, the experts note.

Still, the growth of the global clean energy industry in the upcoming years will be driven by China’s massive, cost-competitive manufacturing capacity for solar PV and wind.

For example, S&P Global projects that as the rest of the world slows down, China will get serious about green hydrogen, with domestic deployment and exports set to grow exponentially in 2026. Furthermore, the country will continue to drive the exponential growth of the global EV industry, with electric vehicle sales set to climb further in 2026.

On a longer horizon, the IEA forecasts that China will continue to account for almost 60% of global renewable capacity growth and is on track to reach its recently announced 2035 wind and solar target five years ahead of schedule.

In total, Asia is poised to dominate the solar PV market with over 48.9% market share by 2026. This would result from surging energy demand in nations such as China and India. Furthermore, China’s data centre demand will more than double, growing from 20 GW in 2024 to potentially 63 GW by 2035.

Experts also note that cooperation between ASEAN and China will continue to expand, particularly in sectors such as technology, digitalisation and green energy, under the ASEAN-China Plan of Action (2026-2030).

S&P Global projects that Asia will drive cleantech spending through 2030.

According to the World Economic Forum, as geopolitical tensions put energy security back at the top of the agenda, China will continue its efforts to bolster the resilience of its energy infrastructure and grow its dominance in new technologies. The nation will continue leading manufacturing across most clean and advanced energy supply chains, cementing its status as the world’s clean energy powerhouse.

China to Further Leverage Clean Energy Leadership in 2026 in the Absence of the US

In 2026, China’s role on the global clean energy stage will be further cemented as the US backtracks on its climate commitments and accelerates its pushback against renewables. In fact, Deloitte projects that the Trump administration will disrupt the country’s clean energy industry, including raising project costs, compressing timelines, tightening discipline among project investors, and disrupting technology imports. This is likely to ease supply chain capacity and any existing gluts, enabling other markets to accelerate planned projects, with China again shaping up as a key supplier and growth market.

S&P Global sees the strategic energy divide between China and the US widening further in 2026 as the former consolidates its leadership in clean energy technologies and supply chains and reinforces its influence through state-led industrial policy and active climate diplomacy. The experts also note that China’s cleantech overcapacity and weakening domestic demand are likely to make the export of cleantech products an economic imperative and a tool for geopolitical power projection.

United States Will Contnue Prioritising Fossil Fuels

The US, on the other hand, will continue prioritising fossil fuel exports and build on its efforts to undermine climate action, including quitting the Paris Agreement, skipping COP30, revoking environmental laws and challenging multilateral efforts such as the International Maritime Organisation (IMO) shipping emissions pricing. Furthermore, after the arrest of Venezuela’s President Nicolas Maduro, the Trump administration announced that US oil companies are about to invest “billions of dollars” to tap into Venezuela’s oil reserves. According to the US Energy Information Administration, Venezuela, a founding member of OPEC, holds the largest proven oil reserves in the world at 303 billion barrels or 17% of the global total. As a result, Trump’s leadership is likely not only to continue but significantly expand its bullish fossil fuel exploration plans, further distancing the US from the global climate goals and fuelling the climate crisis, which the president has previously branded as an “expensive hoax”.

This growing division between the US and China will allow emerging markets to evaluate the differences between the two approaches and choose their own energy transition pathways. According to S&P Global, China’s offering aligns more closely with long-term climate strategies, even as export controls on rare earths highlight supply chain vulnerabilities.

Furthermore, in 2026, China is likely to continue to position itself as an active participant in climate negotiations, building on its role since the Paris Agreement and having recently released new emissions targets. The country is likely to continue engaging in productive dialogues with partners, such as the talks with the EU in July 2025, which marked a new era in climate leadership.

Renewables Growth Still Insufficient to Offset Soaring Electricity Demand

The IEA projects global electricity demand to rise 3.7% in 2026, driven mainly by China and India. Demand in China is forecast to grow by 5.7% in 2026, while India’s is projected to grow by 6.6% this year.

Despite the steady growth trajectory for the clean energy industry, Wood Mackenzie’s Energy Transition Outlook 2025-2026 finds that it would be an insufficient countermeasure to the soaring global power demand, driven mainly by the surge in AI and data centres, which have received special attention at COP30. According to the experts, factors like these have made achieving net zero by 2050 goalunattainable, and are instead driving the world towards catastrophic 2.6°C warming. S&P Global even warns that, as AI uptake soars in 2026, energy supply and sustainability commitments will face a major breaking point.

However, tech giants can still slow down or reverse this trend. For example, at the start of 2026, Google announced it would acquire the clean power and storage developer Intersect for nearly USD 5 billion. The move will enable the company to ensure the clean energy needed to scale AI faster and gain a more competitive position in the global arms race.

However, for this to happen at the pace required by the climate crisis, grid modernisation and buildup would prove critical. As it has become clear that grid development is no longer just an enabler but a critical infrastructure, experts believe the market segment will attract increased investment, further enabling the rapid deployment of renewables. According to S&P Global, 2026 will send the clear message to policymakers, utilities and investors that the energy expansion required to satisfy AI-driven demand growth will only move as fast as the grid allows.

Still, despite the positive trends and the IEA’s expectations for a slight decline in carbon emissions in 2026, the magnitude of the climate crisis demands more urgent and ambitious action.

Looking Ahead

Despite the IEA’s forecasts that the global clean energy capacity will double by 2030, the COP28 goal to triple clean energy capacity is likely to remain out of reach.

However, predictions for the renewables market growth, and solar in particular, always come with caution, with analysts often underestimating its trajectory. For example, policy changes and geopolitics can quickly alter the outlook, as evident in the US and Japan. On the other hand, China’s growing investments in the clean energy market, as well as its consistent actions to crush national targets, can bring back balance in the market elasticity. While these forecasts are hard to nail down, experts are clear: the world is getting closer to the peak in fossil fuel use.

Renewables, led by solar, have been proving the cheapest power source for years already, and are becoming ever more reliable and capable of meeting the rising energy demand.

Moreover, the growing cumulative economic effects of climate hazards, including lost business revenue, physical damage to infrastructure and health impacts, would further accelerate this transition.

Furthermore, as governments increasingly realise the importance of energy security in an increasingly divided geopolitical environment, there is yet another critical reason to accelerate clean energy investments on top of the economic, environmental, and health advantages of renewables. And, with that, the market’s trajectory seems quite clear. The question is: at what pace will it unfold?

Source: Energy Tracker Asia

Huaneng Power International, Inc. Reports Preliminary Consolidated Electricity Sales Results for the First Quarter Ended March 31, 2026

Huaneng Power International, Inc. reported preliminary consolidated electricity sales results for the first quarter ended March 31, 2026. For the quarter, the electricity sold by the Company's operating power plants within the PRC, on consolidated basis, amounted to 101.489 billion kWh, representing a decrease of 4.82% over the same period last year.

https://www.marketscreener.com/news/huaneng-power-international-inc-reports-preliminary-consolidated-electricity-sales-results-for-the-ce7e50dcdd8bf424

Shenergy Company Limited (SSE:600642)

Shanghai Shenergy Power Technology Co., Ltd. (hereinafter referred to as “Shenergy Technology”), the support organization of Shanghai Engineering Research Center of Clean Coal Power, Is a holding subsidiary of Shenergy Company Limited (hereinafter referred to as “Shenergy Co., Ltd.”)

Shenergy Technology is mainly engaged in technology research and development, consulting and services, related to comprehensive retrofit for energy saving and emission reduction, diagnosis of energy saving, and other energy saving and environmental protection work in coal-fired power generation industry. We provide power plant owners with customizes solutions for saving coal, electricity and fuel, comprehensive upgrading retrofit of existing units such as realizing deep peak regulation, and comprehensive optimization of energy saving and emission reduction of new-built units. Meanwhile, Shenergy Technology has been making constant efforts in research, development and innovation of thermal power’s energy saving and emission reduction low-carbon technologies, promoting the advanced concept of “low-carbon, efficient, clean, environment-friendly, highly reliable and deep load regulating” thermal power units to the whole industry.

https://www.shsnpt.com/main/enabout2_1.html

26/4/2026

Shenergy Company Limited Reports Earnings Results for the First Quarter Ended March 31, 2026

Shenergy Company Limited reported earnings results for the first quarter ended March 31, 2026. For the first quarter, the company reported sales was CNY 6,818.15 million compared to CNY 7,337.46 million a year ago. Net income was CNY 787.91 million compared to CNY 1,010.76 million a year ago.

Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was CNY 0.156 compared to CNY 0.207 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was CNY 0.156 compared to CNY 0.207 a year ago.

https://www.marketscreener.com/news/shenergy-company-limited-reports-earnings-results-for-the-first-quarter-ended-march-31-2026-ce7f59dcda88f224

State Grid Corporation of China

State Grid was established in 2002 to carry out state-authorized investments and as a pilot state holding company. As one of the largest utilities in the world, State Grid ranks second on the Fortune Global 500. State Grid constructs and operates power grids as its core business. Its mission is to provide safer, cleaner and more economical and sustainable power. State Grid has RMB536.3 billion in registered capital and 1.72 million employees, providing power to 1.1 billion people in 26 provinces, autonomous regions and municipalities.

https://www.weforum.org/organizations/state-grid-corporation-of-china/

urn:contentItem:6JHV-MVT3-RS20-C0JJ-00000-00Wilson Sonsini Adds Ken Sanocki to Energy and Climate Solutions Practice2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:40:07ZLexisNexisBUSINESS SERVICESFinancial servicesWilson Sonsini Adds Ken Sanocki to Energy and Climate Solutions Practice

Wilson Sonsini Goodrich & Rosati announced that Ken Sanocki has joined as a partner in its Energy and Climate Solutions practice in San Francisco.

Wilson Sonsini Goodrich & Rosati, the premier provider of legal services to technology, life sciences, and growth enterprises worldwide, today announced that Ken Sanocki has joined the firm as a partner in its Energy and Climate Solutions (ECS) practice in San Francisco. His addition enhances the firm's ability to advise clients across the full lifecycle of energy infrastructure financing-from early-stage development through complex capital markets transactions. Sanocki joins from Sunrun, the nation's leading residential solar and storage provider, where he served as vice president of legal.

In that role, he oversaw all legal aspects of project finance, including tax equity financings, tax credit sale agreements, securitizations, warehouse facilities, and other complex debt facilities supporting large-scale distributed energy deployment. During his tenure, Sanocki held roles of increasing seniority while leading more than 50 tax equity financings totaling over $10 billion across distributed energy portfolios, with responsibilities spanning the drafting and negotiation of key transaction documents, preparation of critical deliverables, and overall legal oversight of each financing. "Ken has operated at the center of the energy transition, helping shape the financing structures that enabled distributed energy to scale into mainstream infrastructure," said Doug Clark, managing partner of Wilson Sonsini. "He brings deep technical experience in tax equity and structured financings, along with the judgment and commercial instincts clients need in a rapidly evolving market. We are delighted to welcome him to the firm." At Wilson Sonsini, Sanocki will advise companies, sponsors, and investors on structured and project finance across renewable energy, storage, and climate infrastructure. He will also support clients in capital formation and strategic transactions, including the development of portfolios of revenue-generating assets, as well as companies preparing for IPOs or pursuing M&A activity. "I'm excited to join Wilson Sonsini's ECS practice, which has built a market-leading platform at the convergence of energy innovation, market-opening regulatory advocacy, and capital formation," said Sanocki. "The team has created something truly distinctive, and I look forward to working alongside them to help clients navigate complex financings and scale the next generation of energy infrastructure." Prior to Sunrun, Sanocki served as senior counsel for global structured and project finance at SunEdison and began his career as a project finance associate at Milbank. He earned his J.D. from New York University School of Law and his B.A. from Boston College. Sanocki joins following last year's additions of partners Stanford (Stan) Renas, who focuses on structured finance and securitization transactions, and Matt Christiansen, the most recent former general counsel of the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission. Together, these hires strengthen Wilson Sonsini's ECS platform and expand its capabilities in sophisticated, asset-based energy transition finance, reinforcing the firm's position as a market leader in clean energy and infrastructure advisory.

urn:contentItem:6JHC-G413-RT9J-208B-00000-00CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC - INDUSTRY HIGHLIGHTS INCLUDING PEERS2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:40:07ZLexisNexisSTOCKCLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC - INDUSTRY HIGHLIGHTS INCLUDING PEERS

DASHBOARD: CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC

PermID4296619307Websitehttp://www.clarkconstruction.comIndustryConstructionAddress18201 Von Karman Avenue Suite 800 Irvine, California 92612

ACTIVITIES:

Clark Construction Group, LLC, founded in 1906, is a leading general contractor headquartered in Bethesda, Maryland. With over 4,000 employees across 20+ offices, it serves commercial, infrastructure, and institutional sectors, delivering projects like stadiums, hospitals, and transit systems. Generating over $5 billion annually, Clark is known for its use of Building Information Modeling (BIM) and sustainable practices. In 2024, it completed major infrastructure projects, emphasizing green construction. Its culture prioritizes safety, diversity, and employee development, earning industry accolades. Competing with Turner Construction, Clark stands out for its large-scale project expertise and client partnerships. Recent initiatives include expanding renewable energy construction and digital tools for project management.

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC [4 Press Releases]

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC GREEN FROM THE INSIDE OUT: BERKELEY LAB SETS NEW STANDARDS

Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory's Biological and Environmental Program Integration Center sets new sustainability standards. Berkeley Lab Biological and Environmental Program Integration Center (BioEPIC) unites scientists from LBNL's biosciences and earth and environmental sciences areas in a four-story, 71,000-square-foot facility that sets a new standard for sustainability. Completed in March, the facility offers a suite of next-generation tools designed to study how microbial communities interact with plants and soil to influence the environment.

Inside, 200 researchers perform carefully controlled experiments on microbe-soil-plant interactions with instruments and computing infrastructure that connect BioEPIC to its field research sites. These unique climate research environments, along with three additional levels of labs and collaborative spaces, are housed in an all-electric facility designed to maximize energy efficiency and resiliency.

By supporting an array of unique research needs in this highly sustainable setting, the BioEPIC

Source: Company Website

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC BUILDERS AT HEART: WINSTON VAN KEULEN

This article is part of our series Builders at Heart, where we highlight the passions and backgrounds of the Clark team - the things that shape us - that allow us to tackle challenges head-on, solve complex problems, and build what matters.

Meet Winston van Keulen, a superintendent with Clark Construction, currently working in San Diego. Discover how he is progressing in his role and the keys to success he shares with new team members.

Source: Company Website

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC FROM CONCEPT TO REALITY: HARNESSING 3D PRINTING AT CLARK'S MID-ATLANTIC YARD

Clark Construction's Mid-Atlantic Yard in Upper Marlboro, Maryland, is a powerhouse of specialized services, from fleet oversight to custom fabrication. This diverse facility is also part of a transformative initiative: the integration of 3D printing for prototyping innovative custom solutions to solve complex challenges.

The method came to life when faced with a challenge: designing a custom cleaning piece for a drill rig auger. While the traditional route would involve costly and time-consuming steel fabrication for a prototype, Charles Harman III, a craft and machine shop supervisor, and his team explored a new solution for this type of fabrication and successfully used a 3D printer to create a scaled model. The successful prototype demonstrated the efficacy of the system, and once the 3D printed model was approved, the team began assembling the steel-fabricated version.

Source: Company Website

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC ENHANCING RICHMOND'S WATER RESILIENCE

Clark Water is currently leading improvements on the Shockoe Diversion Structure in Richmond - a key part of the city's combined sewer overflow (CSO) system, which was originally built in the early 1970s. The project will alleviate sewage and stormwater overflow during high-water events. To achieve this, crews have installed mechanical trash rake screens to prevent debris from passing through, two crest gates to minimize upstream flooding, and are making upgrades to supporting infrastructure.

Source: Company Website

+ TURNER SELECTED FOR $500 MILLION WESTCOURT ORLANDO MIXED-USE DEVELOPMENT

The 900,000-square-foot project developed by JMA Ventures will create a vibrant destination in downtown Orlando.

Source: Company Website

+ TURNER PROVIDES AI-POWERED SAFETY TOOL TO THE CONSTRUCTION INDUSTRY

Turner is making SafeT Coach, its AI-powered field safety tool, available free of charge to the broader construction industry. The announcement comes during Turner's 22nd annual Safety Week and company-wide Safety Stand-Down, it's largest to date, under the theme "All In Together."

In construction, conditions change frequently, and the traditional safety resources that establish standards-manuals, scheduled training, retrospective reporting-are not always accessible when and where field teams need them most. SafeT Coach was developed to close that gap.

Source: Company Website

urn:contentItem:6JHD-CMN3-S7TP-92JW-00000-00Primoris Services Corporation Shareholders Are Encouraged to Reach Out to Johnson Fistel for More Information About Potentially Recovering Their Losses2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:40:07ZLexisNexisCLASS ACTIONLAW & LEGAL ISSUESPrimoris Services Corporation Shareholders Are Encouraged to Reach Out to Johnson Fistel for More Information About Potentially Recovering Their Losses

SAN DIEGO, May 06, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Johnson Fistel, PLLP is investigating potential claims on behalf of investors of Primoris Services Corporation (NYSE: PRIM). The investigation focuses on Primoris’s executive officers and whether investor losses may be recovered under federal securities laws.

What if I purchased Primoris securities?

If you purchased Primoris securities and suffered losses on your investment, join our investigation now: Click here to join the investigation.

Or for more information, contact Jim Baker at jimb@johnsonfistel.com or (619) 814-4471.

There is no cost or obligation to you.

Background of the investigation

On May 5, 2026, Primoris reported its first quarter 2026 financial results and updated its full-year outlook. Among other things, the Company disclosed revenue of $1.6 billion, down 5.4% compared to the prior-year period, and net income of $17.4 million, compared to $44.2 million in the prior-year period.

Primoris also disclosed adjusted EBITDA of $60.5 million, down 39.1% compared to the first quarter of 2025, and adjusted EPS of $0.59, compared to $0.98 in the prior-year period.

In addition, Primoris disclosed that revenue in its Energy segment decreased by $152.9 million, or 13.8%, compared to the prior-year period. The Company attributed the decrease primarily to lower renewable energy activity due to slower-than-anticipated starts of new projects, release of new work, and financial close associated with certain projects.

Primoris further disclosed that Energy segment operating income decreased by $49.1 million, or 62.2%, compared to the prior-year period, due to decreased revenue and increased costs on certain renewable energy projects. The Company stated that these higher costs were driven in part by project redesign efforts, changes in project sequencing, labor productivity challenges, and unfavorable weather conditions. Energy gross profit as a percentage of revenue declined to 7.6%, compared to 10.7% in the prior-year period.

Primoris also reduced its full-year 2026 outlook, including adjusted EPS guidance of $4.80 to $5.00, compared to prior guidance of $5.80 to $6.00, and adjusted EBITDA guidance of $480 million to $500 million, compared to prior guidance of $560 million to $580 million.

Following this disclosure, Primoris’s stock price declined sharply, damaging investors.

In light of this disclosure, Johnson Fistel is investigating whether Primoris complied with the federal securities laws. If you suffered losses from your investment in Primoris stock, contact Johnson Fistel.

About Johnson Fistel, PLLP | Securities Fraud & Investor Rights

Johnson Fistel, PLLP is a nationally recognized shareholder-rights law firm with offices in California, New York, Georgia, Idaho, and Colorado. The firm represents individual and institutional investors in shareholder derivative and securities class action lawsuits and also assists foreign investors who purchased shares on U.S. exchanges. To learn more, visit www.johnsonfistel.com.

Achievements

In 2024, Johnson Fistel was ranked among the Top 10 Plaintiff Law Firms by ISS Securities Class Action Services, reflecting the firm’s effectiveness in advocating for investors and recovering approximately $90,725,000 for clients in cases where it served as lead or co-lead counsel.

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JH9-GYY3-S9NN-T0V9-00000-00California investigates Trump administration's deal to end an offshore wind project2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:40:07ZLexisNexisENVIRONMENTCalifornia investigates Trump administration's deal to end an offshore wind projectJENNIFER McDERMOTT

California is investigating one of the Trump administration's deals to end an offshore wind project.

Golden State Wind was a floating offshore wind project proposed off California's central coast. The California Energy Commission said Monday it issued an administrative subpoena to Golden State Wind.

The commission said it is seeking documents and information about the company's recent agreement with the Department of Interior to accept a payout in exchange for voluntarily abandoning its offshore wind lease.

"The Trump administration is recklessly spending billions of taxpayer dollars on backroom deals that would turn back the clock on innovation," CEC Chair David Hochschild said in a statement. "Californians deserve immediate answers about the nature of this payout. Taxpayer dollars should be used to build a sustainable energy future, not to pay to make projects disappear."

The Trump administration is spending nearly $2 billion to get energy companies to walk away from U.S. offshore wind projects. Interior Secretary Doug Burgum said companies were sold a product that was only viable when propped up by massive taxpayer subsidies when they bid for these offshore wind leases in 2022, under former President Joe Biden.

The Republican administration adopted this strategy after federal courts thwarted President Donald Trump's efforts to stop offshore wind development through executive action. Three agreements have been announced.

Under the first deal, made public in March, French company TotalEnergies is getting $1 billion essentially a refund of its leases for offshore wind projects off North Carolina and New York if it invests the money in fossil fuel projects instead. In the latest deals announced last week, the administration said Golden State Wind and Bluepoint Wind agreed to end their leases in exchange for reimbursements totaling nearly $900 million, provided they invest equally in fossil fuels.

Both Golden State and Bluepoint are co-owned by Ocean Winds, a joint venture of EDP Renewables and French energy giant Engie. Bluepoint Wind was an offshore wind project in the early stages of development off the coasts of New Jersey and New York.

When asked about the subpoena Monday, Ocean Winds said it does not comment on open or potential litigation.

This investigation sets the stage for legal action from California to safeguard renewable energy, as well as the thousands of jobs and millions of dollars of investment the state was counting on, said Eddie Ahn, executive director of Brightline Defense, an environmental justice nonprofit working to advance offshore wind in California.

A letter from California Attorney General Rob Bonta's office to Golden State Wind says the state anticipates potential litigation involving the federal government and parties to lease buyouts impacting California's energy needs and offshore wind programs. California has invested about $100 million to support offshore wind development in order to accelerate the state's transition to clean energy and address climate change.

Democrats in Congress are investigating, too. U.S. Reps. Jared Huffman of California, the top Democrat on the House Natural Resources Committee, and Jamie Raskin, the ranking Democrat on the House Judiciary Committee, are demanding information about TotalEnergies agreement.

value.Jurisdiction: California; Massachusetts; New Jersey; New York; North Carolina
value.Title: California investigates Trump administration's deal to end an offshore wind project

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JH9-GYY3-S9GF-H566-00000-00California investigates Trump administration's deal to end an offshore wind project2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:40:07ZLexisNexisNATIONAL POLITICSCalifornia investigates Trump administration's deal to end an offshore wind projectJENNIFER McDERMOTT

California is investigating one of the Trump administration's deals to end an offshore wind project.

Golden State Wind was a floating offshore wind project proposed off California's central coast. The California Energy Commission said Monday it issued an administrative subpoena to Golden State Wind.

The commission said it is seeking documents and information about the company's recent agreement with the Department of Interior to accept a payout in exchange for voluntarily abandoning its offshore wind lease.

"The Trump administration is recklessly spending billions of taxpayer dollars on backroom deals that would turn back the clock on innovation," CEC Chair David Hochschild said in a statement. "Californians deserve immediate answers about the nature of this payout. Taxpayer dollars should be used to build a sustainable energy future, not to pay to make projects disappear."

The Trump administration is spending nearly $2 billion to get energy companies to walk away from U.S. offshore wind projects. Interior Secretary Doug Burgum said companies were sold a product that was only viable when propped up by massive taxpayer subsidies when they bid for these offshore wind leases in 2022, under former President Joe Biden.

The Republican administration adopted this strategy after federal courts thwarted President Donald Trump's efforts to stop offshore wind development through executive action. Three agreements have been announced.

Under the first deal, made public in March, French company TotalEnergies is getting $1 billion essentially a refund of its leases for offshore wind projects off North Carolina and New York if it invests the money in fossil fuel projects instead. In the latest deals announced last week, the administration said Golden State Wind and Bluepoint Wind agreed to end their leases in exchange for reimbursements totaling nearly $900 million, provided they invest equally in fossil fuels.

Both Golden State and Bluepoint are co-owned by Ocean Winds, a joint venture of EDP Renewables and French energy giant Engie. Bluepoint Wind was an offshore wind project in the early stages of development off the coasts of New Jersey and New York.

When asked about the subpoena Monday, Ocean Winds said it does not comment on open or potential litigation.

This investigation sets the stage for legal action from California to safeguard renewable energy, as well as the thousands of jobs and millions of dollars of investment the state was counting on, said Eddie Ahn, executive director of Brightline Defense, an environmental justice nonprofit working to advance offshore wind in California.

A letter from California Attorney General Rob Bonta's office to Golden State Wind says the state anticipates potential litigation involving the federal government and parties to lease buyouts impacting California's energy needs and offshore wind programs. California has invested about $100 million to support offshore wind development in order to accelerate the state's transition to clean energy and address climate change.

Democrats in Congress are investigating, too. U.S. Reps. Jared Huffman of California, the top Democrat on the House Natural Resources Committee, and Jamie Raskin, the ranking Democrat on the House Judiciary Committee, are demanding information about TotalEnergies agreement.

value.Title: California investigates Trump administration's deal to end an offshore wind project
value.Overview: Daily Freeman (Kingston, New York), NATIONAL POLITICS, 599words

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JH9-GYY3-S9GF-H4SW-00000-00California investigates Trump administration's deal to end an offshore wind project2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:40:07ZLexisNexisNATIONAL POLITICSCalifornia investigates Trump administration's deal to end an offshore wind projectJENNIFER McDERMOTT

California is investigating one of the Trump administration's deals to end an offshore wind project.

Golden State Wind was a floating offshore wind project proposed off California's central coast. The California Energy Commission said Monday it issued an administrative subpoena to Golden State Wind.

The commission said it is seeking documents and information about the company's recent agreement with the Department of Interior to accept a payout in exchange for voluntarily abandoning its offshore wind lease.

"The Trump administration is recklessly spending billions of taxpayer dollars on backroom deals that would turn back the clock on innovation," CEC Chair David Hochschild said in a statement. "Californians deserve immediate answers about the nature of this payout. Taxpayer dollars should be used to build a sustainable energy future, not to pay to make projects disappear."

The Trump administration is spending nearly $2 billion to get energy companies to walk away from U.S. offshore wind projects. Interior Secretary Doug Burgum said companies were sold a product that was only viable when propped up by massive taxpayer subsidies when they bid for these offshore wind leases in 2022, under former President Joe Biden.

The Republican administration adopted this strategy after federal courts thwarted President Donald Trump's efforts to stop offshore wind development through executive action. Three agreements have been announced.

Under the first deal, made public in March, French company TotalEnergies is getting $1 billion essentially a refund of its leases for offshore wind projects off North Carolina and New York if it invests the money in fossil fuel projects instead. In the latest deals announced last week, the administration said Golden State Wind and Bluepoint Wind agreed to end their leases in exchange for reimbursements totaling nearly $900 million, provided they invest equally in fossil fuels.

Both Golden State and Bluepoint are co-owned by Ocean Winds, a joint venture of EDP Renewables and French energy giant Engie. Bluepoint Wind was an offshore wind project in the early stages of development off the coasts of New Jersey and New York.

When asked about the subpoena Monday, Ocean Winds said it does not comment on open or potential litigation.

This investigation sets the stage for legal action from California to safeguard renewable energy, as well as the thousands of jobs and millions of dollars of investment the state was counting on, said Eddie Ahn, executive director of Brightline Defense, an environmental justice nonprofit working to advance offshore wind in California.

A letter from California Attorney General Rob Bonta's office to Golden State Wind says the state anticipates potential litigation involving the federal government and parties to lease buyouts impacting California's energy needs and offshore wind programs. California has invested about $100 million to support offshore wind development in order to accelerate the state's transition to clean energy and address climate change.

Democrats in Congress are investigating, too. U.S. Reps. Jared Huffman of California, the top Democrat on the House Natural Resources Committee, and Jamie Raskin, the ranking Democrat on the House Judiciary Committee, are demanding information about TotalEnergies agreement.

The Associated Press' climate and environmental coverage receives financial support from multiple private foundations. AP is solely responsible for all content. Find AP's standards for working with philanthropies, a list of supporters and funded coverage areas at AP.org.
