Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JH6-TKT3-S1N0-G032-00000-00CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC - RECENT SECTOR HIGHLIGHTS INCLUDING PEERS2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:40:07ZLexisNexisSTOCKCLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC - RECENT SECTOR HIGHLIGHTS INCLUDING PEERS

DASHBOARD: CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC

PermID4296619307Websitehttp://www.clarkconstruction.comIndustryConstructionAddress18201 Von Karman Avenue Suite 800 Irvine, California 92612

ACTIVITIES:

Clark Construction Group, LLC, founded in 1906, is a leading general contractor headquartered in Bethesda, Maryland. With over 4,000 employees across 20+ offices, it serves commercial, infrastructure, and institutional sectors, delivering projects like stadiums, hospitals, and transit systems. Generating over $5 billion annually, Clark is known for its use of Building Information Modeling (BIM) and sustainable practices. In 2024, it completed major infrastructure projects, emphasizing green construction. Its culture prioritizes safety, diversity, and employee development, earning industry accolades. Competing with Turner Construction, Clark stands out for its large-scale project expertise and client partnerships. Recent initiatives include expanding renewable energy construction and digital tools for project management.

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC [4 Press Releases]

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC GREEN FROM THE INSIDE OUT: BERKELEY LAB SETS NEW STANDARDS

Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory's Biological and Environmental Program Integration Center sets new sustainability standards. Berkeley Lab Biological and Environmental Program Integration Center (BioEPIC) unites scientists from LBNL's biosciences and earth and environmental sciences areas in a four-story, 71,000-square-foot facility that sets a new standard for sustainability. Completed in March, the facility offers a suite of next-generation tools designed to study how microbial communities interact with plants and soil to influence the environment.

Inside, 200 researchers perform carefully controlled experiments on microbe-soil-plant interactions with instruments and computing infrastructure that connect BioEPIC to its field research sites. These unique climate research environments, along with three additional levels of labs and collaborative spaces, are housed in an all-electric facility designed to maximize energy efficiency and resiliency.

By supporting an array of unique research needs in this highly sustainable setting, the BioEPIC

Source: Company Website

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC BUILDERS AT HEART: WINSTON VAN KEULEN

This article is part of our series Builders at Heart, where we highlight the passions and backgrounds of the Clark team - the things that shape us - that allow us to tackle challenges head-on, solve complex problems, and build what matters.

Meet Winston van Keulen, a superintendent with Clark Construction, currently working in San Diego. Discover how he is progressing in his role and the keys to success he shares with new team members.

Source: Company Website

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC FROM CONCEPT TO REALITY: HARNESSING 3D PRINTING AT CLARK'S MID-ATLANTIC YARD

Clark Construction's Mid-Atlantic Yard in Upper Marlboro, Maryland, is a powerhouse of specialized services, from fleet oversight to custom fabrication. This diverse facility is also part of a transformative initiative: the integration of 3D printing for prototyping innovative custom solutions to solve complex challenges.

The method came to life when faced with a challenge: designing a custom cleaning piece for a drill rig auger. While the traditional route would involve costly and time-consuming steel fabrication for a prototype, Charles Harman III, a craft and machine shop supervisor, and his team explored a new solution for this type of fabrication and successfully used a 3D printer to create a scaled model. The successful prototype demonstrated the efficacy of the system, and once the 3D printed model was approved, the team began assembling the steel-fabricated version.

Source: Company Website

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC ENHANCING RICHMOND'S WATER RESILIENCE

Clark Water is currently leading improvements on the Shockoe Diversion Structure in Richmond - a key part of the city's combined sewer overflow (CSO) system, which was originally built in the early 1970s. The project will alleviate sewage and stormwater overflow during high-water events. To achieve this, crews have installed mechanical trash rake screens to prevent debris from passing through, two crest gates to minimize upstream flooding, and are making upgrades to supporting infrastructure.

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION [3 Press Releases]

+ TURNER PROVIDES AI-POWERED SAFETY TOOL TO THE CONSTRUCTION INDUSTRY

Turner is making SafeT Coach, its AI-powered field safety tool, available free of charge to the broader construction industry. The announcement comes during Turner's 22nd annual Safety Week and company-wide Safety Stand-Down, it's largest to date, under the theme "All In Together."

In construction, conditions change frequently, and the traditional safety resources that establish standards-manuals, scheduled training, retrospective reporting-are not always accessible when and where field teams need them most. SafeT Coach was developed to close that gap.

Source: Company Website

The groundbreaking ceremony held at the new site in Brook Park today marks the official start of construction on Northeast Ohio's largest economic development project to date, the new Huntington Bank Field enclosed stadium and surrounding district.

Turner's support to the Paraplegic Foundation reflects a focus on improving everyday spaces at Sanjeevandeep Special School. urn:contentItem:6JH0-VC63-RX36-R37X-00000-00CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC - THE RECENT PEER NEWS2026-05-04T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:40:07ZLexisNexisSTOCKCLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC - THE RECENT PEER NEWS

DASHBOARD: CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC

PermID4296619307Websitehttp://www.clarkconstruction.comIndustryConstructionAddress18201 Von Karman Avenue Suite 800 Irvine, California 92612

ACTIVITIES:

Clark Construction Group, LLC, founded in 1906, is a leading general contractor headquartered in Bethesda, Maryland. With over 4,000 employees across 20+ offices, it serves commercial, infrastructure, and institutional sectors, delivering projects like stadiums, hospitals, and transit systems. Generating over $5 billion annually, Clark is known for its use of Building Information Modeling (BIM) and sustainable practices. In 2024, it completed major infrastructure projects, emphasizing green construction. Its culture prioritizes safety, diversity, and employee development, earning industry accolades. Competing with Turner Construction, Clark stands out for its large-scale project expertise and client partnerships. Recent initiatives include expanding renewable energy construction and digital tools for project management.

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC [4 Press Releases]

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC GREEN FROM THE INSIDE OUT: BERKELEY LAB SETS NEW STANDARDS

Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory's Biological and Environmental Program Integration Center sets new sustainability standards. Berkeley Lab Biological and Environmental Program Integration Center (BioEPIC) unites scientists from LBNL's biosciences and earth and environmental sciences areas in a four-story, 71,000-square-foot facility that sets a new standard for sustainability. Completed in March, the facility offers a suite of next-generation tools designed to study how microbial communities interact with plants and soil to influence the environment.

Inside, 200 researchers perform carefully controlled experiments on microbe-soil-plant interactions with instruments and computing infrastructure that connect BioEPIC to its field research sites. These unique climate research environments, along with three additional levels of labs and collaborative spaces, are housed in an all-electric facility designed to maximize energy efficiency and resiliency.

By supporting an array of unique research needs in this highly sustainable setting, the BioEPIC

Source: Company Website

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC BUILDERS AT HEART: WINSTON VAN KEULEN

This article is part of our series Builders at Heart, where we highlight the passions and backgrounds of the Clark team - the things that shape us - that allow us to tackle challenges head-on, solve complex problems, and build what matters.

Meet Winston van Keulen, a superintendent with Clark Construction, currently working in San Diego. Discover how he is progressing in his role and the keys to success he shares with new team members.

Source: Company Website

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC FROM CONCEPT TO REALITY: HARNESSING 3D PRINTING AT CLARK'S MID-ATLANTIC YARD

Clark Construction's Mid-Atlantic Yard in Upper Marlboro, Maryland, is a powerhouse of specialized services, from fleet oversight to custom fabrication. This diverse facility is also part of a transformative initiative: the integration of 3D printing for prototyping innovative custom solutions to solve complex challenges.

The method came to life when faced with a challenge: designing a custom cleaning piece for a drill rig auger. While the traditional route would involve costly and time-consuming steel fabrication for a prototype, Charles Harman III, a craft and machine shop supervisor, and his team explored a new solution for this type of fabrication and successfully used a 3D printer to create a scaled model. The successful prototype demonstrated the efficacy of the system, and once the 3D printed model was approved, the team began assembling the steel-fabricated version.

Source: Company Website

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC ENHANCING RICHMOND'S WATER RESILIENCE

Clark Water is currently leading improvements on the Shockoe Diversion Structure in Richmond - a key part of the city's combined sewer overflow (CSO) system, which was originally built in the early 1970s. The project will alleviate sewage and stormwater overflow during high-water events. To achieve this, crews have installed mechanical trash rake screens to prevent debris from passing through, two crest gates to minimize upstream flooding, and are making upgrades to supporting infrastructure.

Source: Company Website

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION: CONNECTING YOUNG TALENT WITH INDUSTRY LEADERS

As part of our Young Professionals Employee Resource Group initiatives, we hosted the first session of our Coffee Talks with Industry Leaders series, with the participation of Arup Turkiye Country Manager Tulay Hatirnaz, Associate Director Duygu Tokalakoglu, and Associate Aydan Basyazicioglu. Held on April 10 at the Arup Istanbul office, the session brought together our young professionals from across Turkiye. Our esteemed speakers shared their industry experience, career journeys, and leadership perspectives, while also engaging with participants through an interactive Q&A.

urn:contentItem:6JH9-NGK3-SFD6-32TV-00000-00Quarterly Report Analysis Quarter ending 31 March 2026: PG&E EPS advances 34% Corporate Wire Date: 24 April 2026 09:28 EDT Release Date: April 23, 20262026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:40:07ZLexisNexisQuarterly Report Analysis Quarter ending 31 March 2026: PG&E EPS advances 34% Corporate Wire Date: 24 April 2026 09:28 EDT Release Date: April 23, 2026

AMERICAN RESULTS

PG&E (NYSE:PCG), announced EPS of 39.0c for the quarter-ended 31 March 2026 [Q1/2026], up 34% sequentially from 29.0c in the previous quarter [Q4/2025].

Major changes compared with previous corresponding period [Mar 31, 2026 vs Mar 31, 2025]:

Favourable Changes:

- Net profit was up 41.4% from $607.0m to $858.0m

- Total revenue was up 15% from $6.0b to $6.9b

- EPS growth 39.3% from 28.0c to 39.0c

- EBITDA to Revenues was up from 29.6% to 30.1%

- Profit before tax to Revenues was up from 11.2% to 13.2%

- EBIT to Revenues was up from 11.2% to 13.2%

- Profit after tax to Revenues was up from 10.1% to 12.5%

Note:

- Depreciation to Revenues was down from 18.3% to 16.9%

Quarterly Report (Quarter ending 31 Mar 2026)

Quarter-ended31 Mar 202631 Dec 202530 Sep 2025EPS, c39.029.037.0Sequential growth in EPS %34-2254Revenue, $ billion6.96.86.2Sequential growth in Revenue %1.18.96.0Net Profit, $ million858642823Sequential growth in Net Profit %33.6-22.058

Compared with the Previous Corresponding Period [PCP; Q1/2025], year-over-year [y.o.y.] EPS was up 39.3%, Revenue was up 15% and Net Profit was up 41.4%.

Quarter-ended31 Mar 202631 Mar 2025EPS, c39.028.0PCP growth in EPS %39-18Revenue, $ billion6.96.0PCP growth in Revenue %15.02.1Net Profit, $ million858607PCP growth in Net Profit %41.4-17.1

ACTIVITIES:

PG&E Corporation, a holding company listed on the New York Stock Exchange, operates as a major energy utility provider in California through its subsidiary, Pacific Gas and Electric Company. Its core business activities encompass the generation, transmission, and distribution of electricity from diverse sources, including natural gas, hydroelectric, nuclear, and increasingly renewables, alongside the transportation and delivery of natural gas to residential, commercial, and industrial customers across Northern and Central California. The company's fastest-growing segments include renewable energy integration, such as solar and wind projects, driven by California's aggressive clean energy targets, as well as grid modernization initiatives like advanced metering infrastructure and support for electric vehicle charging networks. These areas are fueled by rising demand for sustainable solutions, regulatory incentives, and technological advancements, positioning PG&E for long-term growth in a transitioning energy landscape.

MARKET POSITION:

PG&E Corporation (NYSE:PCG), the NYSE's 6th largest Conventional Electricity company by market cap, decreased 54.54c (or 3.1%) in the trailing 12 months to April 23, 2026 to close at $16.83. Compared with the S&P 500 Index which has risen 34.4% over the same period, this is a relative price change of -37.6%. Average daily volume of 22.9 million shares in the trailing year was 1.4 times average daily volume in the previous year.

This Week’s News

Reuters - China invokes anti-sanctions law to counter US blacklisting of refiners - 4/5/2026

China has, for the first time, ‌invoked a law targeting companies that comply with foreign sanctions it rejects, escalating a pushback against the U.S. blacklisting of several oil refineries over purchases of Iranian crude.

For the complete story, see:

https://www.reuters.com/business/energy/china-invokes-anti-sanctions-law-counter-us-blacklisting-refiners-2026-05-04/

Solar Paces - Solar experts offer a solution to satisfy both sides in Ivanpah decommissioning battle - 4/5/2026

Two California electric utilities have moved to exit long-term contracts to buy power from Ivanpah, a unique first-of-its-kind utility-scale solar tower project that has delivered only around 70–80% of its projected annual generation since it began operating in 2014.

For the complete story, see:

https://www.solarpaces.org/solar-experts-offer-a-solution-to-satisfy-both-sides-in-ivanpah-decommissioning-battle/

Bastille Post - China launches first green power project integrating computing power, electricity supply in Ningxia - 2/5/2026

The photovoltaic power station with a total installed capacity of 500,000 kilowatts was put into operation to serve the computing power park under the cloud data center of China Datang Corporation Limited in Zhongwei City.

For the complete story, see:

https://www.bastillepost.com/global/article/5825084-china-launches-first-green-power-project-integrating-computing-power-electricity-supply-in-ningxia

urn:contentItem:6JH5-5NH3-RWHT-C503-00000-00Quarterly Report Analysis Quarter ending 31 March 2026: PG&E reports 34% sequential rise in Quarterly Net Profit2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:40:07ZLexisNexisQuarterly Report Analysis Quarter ending 31 March 2026: PG&E reports 34% sequential rise in Quarterly Net Profit

AMERICAN RESULTS

PG&E (NYSE:PCG), announced net profit of $858m for the quarter-ended 31 March 2026 [Q1/2026], up 34% from the previous quarter [Q4/2025] and up 41% from the year-earlier period [Q1/2025].

Major changes compared with previous corresponding period [Mar 31, 2026 vs Mar 31, 2025]:

Favourable Changes:

- Net profit was up 41.4% from $607.0m to $858.0m

- Total revenue was up 15% from $6.0b to $6.9b

- EPS growth 39.3% from 28.0c to 39.0c

- EBITDA to Revenues was up from 29.6% to 30.1%

- Profit before tax to Revenues was up from 11.2% to 13.2%

- EBIT to Revenues was up from 11.2% to 13.2%

- Profit after tax to Revenues was up from 10.1% to 12.5%

Note:

- Depreciation to Revenues was down from 18.3% to 16.9%

Quarterly Report (Quarter ending 31 Mar 2026)

Quarter-ended31 Mar 202631 Dec 202530 Sep 2025EPS, c39.029.037.0Sequential growth in EPS %34-2254Revenue, $ billion6.96.86.2Sequential growth in Revenue %1.18.96.0Net Profit, $ million858642823Sequential growth in Net Profit %33.6-22.058

Compared with the Previous Corresponding Period [PCP; Q1/2025], year-over-year [y.o.y.] EPS was up 39.3%, Revenue was up 15% and Net Profit was up 41.4%.

Quarter-ended31 Mar 202631 Mar 2025EPS, c39.028.0PCP growth in EPS %39-18Revenue, $ billion6.96.0PCP growth in Revenue %15.02.1Net Profit, $ million858607PCP growth in Net Profit %41.4-17.1

ACTIVITIES:

PG&E Corporation, a holding company listed on the New York Stock Exchange, operates as a major energy utility provider in California through its subsidiary, Pacific Gas and Electric Company. Its core business activities encompass the generation, transmission, and distribution of electricity from diverse sources, including natural gas, hydroelectric, nuclear, and increasingly renewables, alongside the transportation and delivery of natural gas to residential, commercial, and industrial customers across Northern and Central California. The company's fastest-growing segments include renewable energy integration, such as solar and wind projects, driven by California's aggressive clean energy targets, as well as grid modernization initiatives like advanced metering infrastructure and support for electric vehicle charging networks. These areas are fueled by rising demand for sustainable solutions, regulatory incentives, and technological advancements, positioning PG&E for long-term growth in a transitioning energy landscape.

MARKET POSITION:

PG&E Corporation (NYSE:PCG), the NYSE's 6th largest Conventional Electricity company by market cap, decreased 54.54c (or 3.1%) in the trailing 12 months to April 23, 2026 to close at $16.83. Compared with the S&P 500 Index which has risen 34.4% over the same period, this is a relative price change of -37.6%. Average daily volume of 22.9 million shares in the trailing year was 1.4 times average daily volume in the previous year.

value.Title: California investigates Trump administration's deal to end an offshore wind project

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JH9-GYY3-S9NN-T120-00000-00California investigates Trump administration's deal to end an offshore wind project2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:40:07ZLexisNexisENVIRONMENTCalifornia investigates Trump administration's deal to end an offshore wind projectJENNIFER McDERMOTT

California is investigating one of the Trump administration's deals to end an offshore wind project.

Golden State Wind was a floating offshore wind project proposed off California's central coast. The California Energy Commission said Monday it issued an administrative subpoena to Golden State Wind.

The commission said it is seeking documents and information about the company's recent agreement with the Department of Interior to accept a payout in exchange for voluntarily abandoning its offshore wind lease.

"The Trump administration is recklessly spending billions of taxpayer dollars on backroom deals that would turn back the clock on innovation," CEC Chair David Hochschild said in a statement. "Californians deserve immediate answers about the nature of this payout. Taxpayer dollars should be used to build a sustainable energy future, not to pay to make projects disappear."

The Trump administration is spending nearly $2 billion to get energy companies to walk away from U.S. offshore wind projects. Interior Secretary Doug Burgum said companies were sold a product that was only viable when propped up by massive taxpayer subsidies when they bid for these offshore wind leases in 2022, under former President Joe Biden.

The Republican administration adopted this strategy after federal courts thwarted President Donald Trump's efforts to stop offshore wind development through executive action. Three agreements have been announced.

Under the first deal, made public in March, French company TotalEnergies is getting $1 billion essentially a refund of its leases for offshore wind projects off North Carolina and New York if it invests the money in fossil fuel projects instead. In the latest deals announced last week, the administration said Golden State Wind and Bluepoint Wind agreed to end their leases in exchange for reimbursements totaling nearly $900 million, provided they invest equally in fossil fuels.

Both Golden State and Bluepoint are co-owned by Ocean Winds, a joint venture of EDP Renewables and French energy giant Engie. Bluepoint Wind was an offshore wind project in the early stages of development off the coasts of New Jersey and New York.

When asked about the subpoena Monday, Ocean Winds said it does not comment on open or potential litigation.

This investigation sets the stage for legal action from California to safeguard renewable energy, as well as the thousands of jobs and millions of dollars of investment the state was counting on, said Eddie Ahn, executive director of Brightline Defense, an environmental justice nonprofit working to advance offshore wind in California.

A letter from California Attorney General Rob Bonta's office to Golden State Wind says the state anticipates potential litigation involving the federal government and parties to lease buyouts impacting California's energy needs and offshore wind programs. California has invested about $100 million to support offshore wind development in order to accelerate the state's transition to clean energy and address climate change.

Democrats in Congress are investigating, too. U.S. Reps. Jared Huffman of California, the top Democrat on the House Natural Resources Committee, and Jamie Raskin, the ranking Democrat on the House Judiciary Committee, are demanding information about TotalEnergies agreement.

urn:contentItem:6JHB-9XG3-RSRS-S52W-00000-00CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC - INDUSTRY IN FOCUS: TRENDS & PEER ACTIVITY2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:40:07ZLexisNexisSTOCKCLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC - INDUSTRY IN FOCUS: TRENDS & PEER ACTIVITY

DASHBOARD: CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC

PermID4296619307Websitehttp://www.clarkconstruction.comIndustryConstructionAddress18201 Von Karman Avenue Suite 800 Irvine, California 92612

ACTIVITIES:

Clark Construction Group, LLC, founded in 1906, is a leading general contractor headquartered in Bethesda, Maryland. With over 4,000 employees across 20+ offices, it serves commercial, infrastructure, and institutional sectors, delivering projects like stadiums, hospitals, and transit systems. Generating over $5 billion annually, Clark is known for its use of Building Information Modeling (BIM) and sustainable practices. In 2024, it completed major infrastructure projects, emphasizing green construction. Its culture prioritizes safety, diversity, and employee development, earning industry accolades. Competing with Turner Construction, Clark stands out for its large-scale project expertise and client partnerships. Recent initiatives include expanding renewable energy construction and digital tools for project management.

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC [4 Press Releases]

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC GREEN FROM THE INSIDE OUT: BERKELEY LAB SETS NEW STANDARDS

Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory's Biological and Environmental Program Integration Center sets new sustainability standards. Berkeley Lab Biological and Environmental Program Integration Center (BioEPIC) unites scientists from LBNL's biosciences and earth and environmental sciences areas in a four-story, 71,000-square-foot facility that sets a new standard for sustainability. Completed in March, the facility offers a suite of next-generation tools designed to study how microbial communities interact with plants and soil to influence the environment.

Inside, 200 researchers perform carefully controlled experiments on microbe-soil-plant interactions with instruments and computing infrastructure that connect BioEPIC to its field research sites. These unique climate research environments, along with three additional levels of labs and collaborative spaces, are housed in an all-electric facility designed to maximize energy efficiency and resiliency.

By supporting an array of unique research needs in this highly sustainable setting, the BioEPIC

Source: Company Website

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC BUILDERS AT HEART: WINSTON VAN KEULEN

This article is part of our series Builders at Heart, where we highlight the passions and backgrounds of the Clark team - the things that shape us - that allow us to tackle challenges head-on, solve complex problems, and build what matters.

Meet Winston van Keulen, a superintendent with Clark Construction, currently working in San Diego. Discover how he is progressing in his role and the keys to success he shares with new team members.

Source: Company Website

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC FROM CONCEPT TO REALITY: HARNESSING 3D PRINTING AT CLARK'S MID-ATLANTIC YARD

Clark Construction's Mid-Atlantic Yard in Upper Marlboro, Maryland, is a powerhouse of specialized services, from fleet oversight to custom fabrication. This diverse facility is also part of a transformative initiative: the integration of 3D printing for prototyping innovative custom solutions to solve complex challenges.

The method came to life when faced with a challenge: designing a custom cleaning piece for a drill rig auger. While the traditional route would involve costly and time-consuming steel fabrication for a prototype, Charles Harman III, a craft and machine shop supervisor, and his team explored a new solution for this type of fabrication and successfully used a 3D printer to create a scaled model. The successful prototype demonstrated the efficacy of the system, and once the 3D printed model was approved, the team began assembling the steel-fabricated version.

Source: Company Website

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC ENHANCING RICHMOND'S WATER RESILIENCE

Clark Water is currently leading improvements on the Shockoe Diversion Structure in Richmond - a key part of the city's combined sewer overflow (CSO) system, which was originally built in the early 1970s. The project will alleviate sewage and stormwater overflow during high-water events. To achieve this, crews have installed mechanical trash rake screens to prevent debris from passing through, two crest gates to minimize upstream flooding, and are making upgrades to supporting infrastructure.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION [1 Press Release]

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION LABOR SHORTAGE LOOMS AS CONSTRUCTION REBOUNDS

SANTA CLARA, Calif., Dec. 9, 2025 - Just as individual market sectors are finding ways to move projects forward after a year of supply chain and cost turbulence, the ongoing labor shortage threatens to bog down construction unless owners and contractors embrace new approaches, according to the latest Market Conditions Report from DPR Construction, one of the nation's top technical builders.

A recent survey by the Associated General Contractors of America and the national Center for Construction Education and Research showed that:

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION [3 Press Releases]

+ TURNER PROVIDES AI-POWERED SAFETY TOOL TO THE CONSTRUCTION INDUSTRY

Turner is making SafeT Coach, its AI-powered field safety tool, available free of charge to the broader construction industry. The announcement comes during Turner's 22nd annual Safety Week and company-wide Safety Stand-Down, it's largest to date, under the theme "All In Together."

In construction, conditions change frequently, and the traditional safety resources that establish standards-manuals, scheduled training, retrospective reporting-are not always accessible when and where field teams need them most. SafeT Coach was developed to close that gap.

Source: Company Website

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION HASLAM SPORTS GROUP, AECOM HUNT AND TURNER JV BREAK GROUND ON HUNTINGTON BANK FIELD

Construction of New State-of-the-Art Facility to Serve as Ohio's First Enclosed Stadium Revolutionary Design by HKS to Bring Football Fans Closer to the Field Than EverThe Haslam Sports Group (HSG) alongside a joint venture of AECOM Hunt and Turner Construction Company and HKS today announced the groundbreaking of the new Huntington Bank Field, the future home of the Cleveland Browns starting in the 2029 season. The groundbreaking ceremony held at the new site in Brook Park today marks the official start of construction on Northeast Ohio's largest economic development project to date, the new Huntington Bank Field enclosed stadium and surrounding district.

Source: Company Website

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION WHERE SMALL CHANGES CREATE REAL IMPACT

Turner's support to the Paraplegic Foundation reflects a focus on improving everyday spaces at Sanjeevandeep Special School. The initiative has contributed to creating a more accessible, comfortable environment for children and caregivers.Our recent association with the Paraplegic Foundation came from a shared belief that everyone deserves the opportunity to live with dignity, comfort, and as much independence as possible. Through their work, the Foundation supports initiatives like Sanjeevandeep Special School, a space dedicated to children with special needs, where care, learning, and rehabilitation come together in a very real, hands-on way.

Source: Company Website

urn:contentItem:6JGY-HMB3-SD84-W3JR-00000-00CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC - INDUSTRY UPDATES FROM PEERS2026-05-04T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:40:07ZLexisNexisSTOCKCLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC - INDUSTRY UPDATES FROM PEERS

DASHBOARD: CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC

PermID4296619307Websitehttp://www.clarkconstruction.comIndustryConstructionAddress18201 Von Karman Avenue Suite 800 Irvine, California 92612

ACTIVITIES:

Clark Construction Group, LLC, founded in 1906, is a leading general contractor headquartered in Bethesda, Maryland. With over 4,000 employees across 20+ offices, it serves commercial, infrastructure, and institutional sectors, delivering projects like stadiums, hospitals, and transit systems. Generating over $5 billion annually, Clark is known for its use of Building Information Modeling (BIM) and sustainable practices. In 2024, it completed major infrastructure projects, emphasizing green construction. Its culture prioritizes safety, diversity, and employee development, earning industry accolades. Competing with Turner Construction, Clark stands out for its large-scale project expertise and client partnerships. Recent initiatives include expanding renewable energy construction and digital tools for project management.

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC [4 Press Releases]

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC GREEN FROM THE INSIDE OUT: BERKELEY LAB SETS NEW STANDARDS

Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory's Biological and Environmental Program Integration Center sets new sustainability standards. Berkeley Lab Biological and Environmental Program Integration Center (BioEPIC) unites scientists from LBNL's biosciences and earth and environmental sciences areas in a four-story, 71,000-square-foot facility that sets a new standard for sustainability. Completed in March, the facility offers a suite of next-generation tools designed to study how microbial communities interact with plants and soil to influence the environment.

Inside, 200 researchers perform carefully controlled experiments on microbe-soil-plant interactions with instruments and computing infrastructure that connect BioEPIC to its field research sites. These unique climate research environments, along with three additional levels of labs and collaborative spaces, are housed in an all-electric facility designed to maximize energy efficiency and resiliency.

By supporting an array of unique research needs in this highly sustainable setting, the BioEPIC

Source: Company Website

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC BUILDERS AT HEART: WINSTON VAN KEULEN

This article is part of our series Builders at Heart, where we highlight the passions and backgrounds of the Clark team - the things that shape us - that allow us to tackle challenges head-on, solve complex problems, and build what matters.

Meet Winston van Keulen, a superintendent with Clark Construction, currently working in San Diego. Discover how he is progressing in his role and the keys to success he shares with new team members.

Source: Company Website

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC FROM CONCEPT TO REALITY: HARNESSING 3D PRINTING AT CLARK'S MID-ATLANTIC YARD

Clark Construction's Mid-Atlantic Yard in Upper Marlboro, Maryland, is a powerhouse of specialized services, from fleet oversight to custom fabrication. This diverse facility is also part of a transformative initiative: the integration of 3D printing for prototyping innovative custom solutions to solve complex challenges.

The method came to life when faced with a challenge: designing a custom cleaning piece for a drill rig auger. While the traditional route would involve costly and time-consuming steel fabrication for a prototype, Charles Harman III, a craft and machine shop supervisor, and his team explored a new solution for this type of fabrication and successfully used a 3D printer to create a scaled model. The successful prototype demonstrated the efficacy of the system, and once the 3D printed model was approved, the team began assembling the steel-fabricated version.

Source: Company Website

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC ENHANCING RICHMOND'S WATER RESILIENCE

Clark Water is currently leading improvements on the Shockoe Diversion Structure in Richmond - a key part of the city's combined sewer overflow (CSO) system, which was originally built in the early 1970s. The project will alleviate sewage and stormwater overflow during high-water events. To achieve this, crews have installed mechanical trash rake screens to prevent debris from passing through, two crest gates to minimize upstream flooding, and are making upgrades to supporting infrastructure.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION [1 Press Release]

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION LABOR SHORTAGE LOOMS AS CONSTRUCTION REBOUNDS

SANTA CLARA, Calif., Dec. 9, 2025 - Just as individual market sectors are finding ways to move projects forward after a year of supply chain and cost turbulence, the ongoing labor shortage threatens to bog down construction unless owners and contractors embrace new approaches, according to the latest Market Conditions Report from DPR Construction, one of the nation's top technical builders.

A recent survey by the Associated General Contractors of America and the national Center for Construction Education and Research showed that:

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION [3 Press Releases]

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION HASLAM SPORTS GROUP, AECOM HUNT AND TURNER JV BREAK GROUND ON HUNTINGTON BANK FIELD

Construction of New State-of-the-Art Facility to Serve as Ohio's First Enclosed Stadium Revolutionary Design by HKS to Bring Football Fans Closer to the Field Than EverThe Haslam Sports Group (HSG) alongside a joint venture of AECOM Hunt and Turner Construction Company and HKS today announced the groundbreaking of the new Huntington Bank Field, the future home of the Cleveland Browns starting in the 2029 season. The groundbreaking ceremony held at the new site in Brook Park today marks the official start of construction on Northeast Ohio's largest economic development project to date, the new Huntington Bank Field enclosed stadium and surrounding district.

Source: Company Website

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION WHERE SMALL CHANGES CREATE REAL IMPACT

Turner's support to the Paraplegic Foundation reflects a focus on improving everyday spaces at Sanjeevandeep Special School. The initiative has contributed to creating a more accessible, comfortable environment for children and caregivers.Our recent association with the Paraplegic Foundation came from a shared belief that everyone deserves the opportunity to live with dignity, comfort, and as much independence as possible. Through their work, the Foundation supports initiatives like Sanjeevandeep Special School, a space dedicated to children with special needs, where care, learning, and rehabilitation come together in a very real, hands-on way.

Source: Company Website

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION: CONNECTING YOUNG TALENT WITH INDUSTRY LEADERS

As part of our Young Professionals Employee Resource Group initiatives, we hosted the first session of our Coffee Talks with Industry Leaders series, with the participation of Arup Turkiye Country Manager Tulay Hatirnaz, Associate Director Duygu Tokalakoglu, and Associate Aydan Basyazicioglu. Held on April 10 at the Arup Istanbul office, the session brought together our young professionals from across Turkiye. Our esteemed speakers shared their industry experience, career journeys, and leadership perspectives, while also engaging with participants through an interactive Q&A.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ TURNER PROVIDES AI-POWERED SAFETY TOOL TO THE CONSTRUCTION INDUSTRY

Turner is making SafeT Coach, its AI-powered field safety tool, available free of charge to the broader construction industry. The announcement comes during Turner's 22nd annual Safety Week and company-wide Safety Stand-Down, it's largest to date, under the theme "All In Together."

In construction, conditions change frequently, and the traditional safety resources that establish standards-manuals, scheduled training, retrospective reporting-are not always accessible when and where field teams need them most. SafeT Coach was developed to close that gap.

urn:contentItem:6JH9-GYY3-S9NN-T0H8-00000-00California investigates Trump administration's deal to end an offshore wind project2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:40:07ZLexisNexisNATIONAL POLITICSCalifornia investigates Trump administration's deal to end an offshore wind projectJENNIFER McDERMOTT

California is investigating one of the Trump administration's deals to end an offshore wind project.

Golden State Wind was a floating offshore wind project proposed off California's central coast. The California Energy Commission said Monday it issued an administrative subpoena to Golden State Wind.

The commission said it is seeking documents and information about the company's recent agreement with the Department of Interior to accept a payout in exchange for voluntarily abandoning its offshore wind lease.

"The Trump administration is recklessly spending billions of taxpayer dollars on backroom deals that would turn back the clock on innovation," CEC Chair David Hochschild said in a statement. "Californians deserve immediate answers about the nature of this payout. Taxpayer dollars should be used to build a sustainable energy future, not to pay to make projects disappear."

The Trump administration is spending nearly $2 billion to get energy companies to walk away from U.S. offshore wind projects. Interior Secretary Doug Burgum said companies were sold a product that was only viable when propped up by massive taxpayer subsidies when they bid for these offshore wind leases in 2022, under former President Joe Biden.

The Republican administration adopted this strategy after federal courts thwarted President Donald Trump's efforts to stop offshore wind development through executive action. Three agreements have been announced.

Under the first deal, made public in March, French company TotalEnergies is getting $1 billion essentially a refund of its leases for offshore wind projects off North Carolina and New York if it invests the money in fossil fuel projects instead. In the latest deals announced last week, the administration said Golden State Wind and Bluepoint Wind agreed to end their leases in exchange for reimbursements totaling nearly $900 million, provided they invest equally in fossil fuels.

Both Golden State and Bluepoint are co-owned by Ocean Winds, a joint venture of EDP Renewables and French energy giant Engie. Bluepoint Wind was an offshore wind project in the early stages of development off the coasts of New Jersey and New York.

When asked about the subpoena Monday, Ocean Winds said it does not comment on open or potential litigation.

This investigation sets the stage for legal action from California to safeguard renewable energy, as well as the thousands of jobs and millions of dollars of investment the state was counting on, said Eddie Ahn, executive director of Brightline Defense, an environmental justice nonprofit working to advance offshore wind in California.

A letter from California Attorney General Rob Bonta's office to Golden State Wind says the state anticipates potential litigation involving the federal government and parties to lease buyouts impacting California's energy needs and offshore wind programs. California has invested about $100 million to support offshore wind development in order to accelerate the state's transition to clean energy and address climate change.

Democrats in Congress are investigating, too. U.S. Reps. Jared Huffman of California, the top Democrat on the House Natural Resources Committee, and Jamie Raskin, the ranking Democrat on the House Judiciary Committee, are demanding information about TotalEnergies agreement.

value.Title: CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC - INDUSTRY SPOTLIGHT: KEY TRENDS AND PEER HIGHLIGHTS

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JH9-NGK3-SFD6-3340-00000-00CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC - INDUSTRY SPOTLIGHT: KEY TRENDS AND PEER HIGHLIGHTS2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:40:07ZLexisNexisSTOCKCLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC - INDUSTRY SPOTLIGHT: KEY TRENDS AND PEER HIGHLIGHTS

DASHBOARD: CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC

PermID4296619307Websitehttp://www.clarkconstruction.comIndustryConstructionAddress18201 Von Karman Avenue Suite 800 Irvine, California 92612

ACTIVITIES:

Clark Construction Group, LLC, founded in 1906, is a leading general contractor headquartered in Bethesda, Maryland. With over 4,000 employees across 20+ offices, it serves commercial, infrastructure, and institutional sectors, delivering projects like stadiums, hospitals, and transit systems. Generating over $5 billion annually, Clark is known for its use of Building Information Modeling (BIM) and sustainable practices. In 2024, it completed major infrastructure projects, emphasizing green construction. Its culture prioritizes safety, diversity, and employee development, earning industry accolades. Competing with Turner Construction, Clark stands out for its large-scale project expertise and client partnerships. Recent initiatives include expanding renewable energy construction and digital tools for project management.

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC [4 Press Releases]

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC GREEN FROM THE INSIDE OUT: BERKELEY LAB SETS NEW STANDARDS

Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory's Biological and Environmental Program Integration Center sets new sustainability standards. Berkeley Lab Biological and Environmental Program Integration Center (BioEPIC) unites scientists from LBNL's biosciences and earth and environmental sciences areas in a four-story, 71,000-square-foot facility that sets a new standard for sustainability. Completed in March, the facility offers a suite of next-generation tools designed to study how microbial communities interact with plants and soil to influence the environment.

Inside, 200 researchers perform carefully controlled experiments on microbe-soil-plant interactions with instruments and computing infrastructure that connect BioEPIC to its field research sites. These unique climate research environments, along with three additional levels of labs and collaborative spaces, are housed in an all-electric facility designed to maximize energy efficiency and resiliency.

By supporting an array of unique research needs in this highly sustainable setting, the BioEPIC

Source: Company Website

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC BUILDERS AT HEART: WINSTON VAN KEULEN

This article is part of our series Builders at Heart, where we highlight the passions and backgrounds of the Clark team - the things that shape us - that allow us to tackle challenges head-on, solve complex problems, and build what matters.

Meet Winston van Keulen, a superintendent with Clark Construction, currently working in San Diego. Discover how he is progressing in his role and the keys to success he shares with new team members.

Source: Company Website

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC FROM CONCEPT TO REALITY: HARNESSING 3D PRINTING AT CLARK'S MID-ATLANTIC YARD

Clark Construction's Mid-Atlantic Yard in Upper Marlboro, Maryland, is a powerhouse of specialized services, from fleet oversight to custom fabrication. This diverse facility is also part of a transformative initiative: the integration of 3D printing for prototyping innovative custom solutions to solve complex challenges.

The method came to life when faced with a challenge: designing a custom cleaning piece for a drill rig auger. While the traditional route would involve costly and time-consuming steel fabrication for a prototype, Charles Harman III, a craft and machine shop supervisor, and his team explored a new solution for this type of fabrication and successfully used a 3D printer to create a scaled model. The successful prototype demonstrated the efficacy of the system, and once the 3D printed model was approved, the team began assembling the steel-fabricated version.

Source: Company Website

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC ENHANCING RICHMOND'S WATER RESILIENCE

Clark Water is currently leading improvements on the Shockoe Diversion Structure in Richmond - a key part of the city's combined sewer overflow (CSO) system, which was originally built in the early 1970s. The project will alleviate sewage and stormwater overflow during high-water events. To achieve this, crews have installed mechanical trash rake screens to prevent debris from passing through, two crest gates to minimize upstream flooding, and are making upgrades to supporting infrastructure.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION [1 Press Release]

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION LABOR SHORTAGE LOOMS AS CONSTRUCTION REBOUNDS

SANTA CLARA, Calif., Dec. 9, 2025 - Just as individual market sectors are finding ways to move projects forward after a year of supply chain and cost turbulence, the ongoing labor shortage threatens to bog down construction unless owners and contractors embrace new approaches, according to the latest Market Conditions Report from DPR Construction, one of the nation's top technical builders.

A recent survey by the Associated General Contractors of America and the national Center for Construction Education and Research showed that:

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION [3 Press Releases]

+ TURNER PROVIDES AI-POWERED SAFETY TOOL TO THE CONSTRUCTION INDUSTRY

Turner is making SafeT Coach, its AI-powered field safety tool, available free of charge to the broader construction industry. The announcement comes during Turner's 22nd annual Safety Week and company-wide Safety Stand-Down, it's largest to date, under the theme "All In Together."

In construction, conditions change frequently, and the traditional safety resources that establish standards-manuals, scheduled training, retrospective reporting-are not always accessible when and where field teams need them most. SafeT Coach was developed to close that gap.

Source: Company Website

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION HASLAM SPORTS GROUP, AECOM HUNT AND TURNER JV BREAK GROUND ON HUNTINGTON BANK FIELD

Construction of New State-of-the-Art Facility to Serve as Ohio's First Enclosed Stadium Revolutionary Design by HKS to Bring Football Fans Closer to the Field Than EverThe Haslam Sports Group (HSG) alongside a joint venture of AECOM Hunt and Turner Construction Company and HKS today announced the groundbreaking of the new Huntington Bank Field, the future home of the Cleveland Browns starting in the 2029 season. The groundbreaking ceremony held at the new site in Brook Park today marks the official start of construction on Northeast Ohio's largest economic development project to date, the new Huntington Bank Field enclosed stadium and surrounding district.

Source: Company Website

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION WHERE SMALL CHANGES CREATE REAL IMPACT

Turner's support to the Paraplegic Foundation reflects a focus on improving everyday spaces at Sanjeevandeep Special School. The initiative has contributed to creating a more accessible, comfortable environment for children and caregivers.Our recent association with the Paraplegic Foundation came from a shared belief that everyone deserves the opportunity to live with dignity, comfort, and as much independence as possible. Through their work, the Foundation supports initiatives like Sanjeevandeep Special School, a space dedicated to children with special needs, where care, learning, and rehabilitation come together in a very real, hands-on way.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION WHERE SMALL CHANGES CREATE REAL IMPACT

Turner's support to the Paraplegic Foundation reflects a focus on improving everyday spaces at Sanjeevandeep Special School. The initiative has contributed to creating a more accessible, comfortable environment for children and caregivers.Our recent association with the Paraplegic Foundation came from a shared belief that everyone deserves the opportunity to live with dignity, comfort, and as much independence as possible. Through their work, the Foundation supports initiatives like Sanjeevandeep Special School, a space dedicated to children with special needs, where care, learning, and rehabilitation come together in a very real, hands-on way.

Source: Company Website

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION: CONNECTING YOUNG TALENT WITH INDUSTRY LEADERS

As part of our Young Professionals Employee Resource Group initiatives, we hosted the first session of our Coffee Talks with Industry Leaders series, with the participation of Arup Turkiye Country Manager Tulay Hatirnaz, Associate Director Duygu Tokalakoglu, and Associate Aydan Basyazicioglu. Held on April 10 at the Arup Istanbul office, the session brought together our young professionals from across Turkiye. Our esteemed speakers shared their industry experience, career journeys, and leadership perspectives, while also engaging with participants through an interactive Q&A.

Source: Company Website

Hanz Christensen

May 4th, 2026 ( Watchlist News - Delivered by Newstex )

Mitsubishi UFJ Trust & Banking Corp lessened its position in World Kinect Corporation ( NYSE:WKC - Free Report) by 21.2% during the fourth quarter, according to the company in its most recent Form 13F filing with the Securities & Exchange Commission. The fund owned 95,736 shares of the company's stock after selling 25,727 shares during the quarter. Mitsubishi UFJ Trust & Banking Corp owned 0.17% of World Kinect worth $2,243,000 as of its most recent filing with the Securities & Exchange Commission.

A number of other institutional investors have also added to or reduced their stakes in the company. Clearstead Advisors LLC increased its stake in World Kinect by 52.8% in the third quarter. Clearstead Advisors LLC now owns 1,261 shares of the company's stock valued at $33,000 after acquiring an additional 436 shares during the last quarter. Johnson Investment Counsel Inc. increased its stake in World Kinect by 0.5% in the third quarter. Johnson Investment Counsel Inc. now owns 110,836 shares of the company's stock valued at $2,876,000 after acquiring an additional 502 shares during the last quarter. PNC Financial Services Group Inc. increased its stake in World Kinect by 8.5% in the third quarter. PNC Financial Services Group Inc. now owns 6,397 shares of the company's stock valued at $166,000 after acquiring an additional 503 shares during the last quarter. GF Fund Management CO. LTD. increased its stake in World Kinect by 5.0% in the fourth quarter. GF Fund Management CO. LTD. now owns 16,135 shares of the company's stock valued at $378,000 after acquiring an additional 771 shares during the last quarter. Finally, California State Teachers Retirement System increased its stake in World Kinect by 1.4% in the second quarter. California State Teachers Retirement System now owns 54,354 shares of the company's stock valued at $1,541,000 after acquiring an additional 775 shares during the last quarter. Institutional investors own 97.06% of the company's stock.

Analyst Upgrades and Downgrades

A number of research analysts recently commented on WKC shares. Wall Street Zen raised World Kinect from a "hold" rating to a "buy" rating in a research note on Saturday. Zacks Research raised World Kinect from a "strong sell" rating to a "hold" rating in a research note on Wednesday, March 25th. Finally, Morgan Stanley reduced their price objective on World Kinect from $27.00 to $25.00 and set an "underweight" rating on the stock in a research note on Tuesday, March 3rd. One analyst has rated the stock with a Buy rating, one has given a Hold rating and two have given a Sell rating to the stock. According to MarketBeat.com, the stock has a consensus rating of "Reduce" and an average target price of $29.00.

Get Our Latest Report on WKC

World Kinect Stock Up 1.2%

Shares of NYSE:WKC opened at $27.43 on Tuesday. The stock's 50-day moving average price is $23.97 and its 200-day moving average price is $24.84. World Kinect Corporation has a 12-month low of $22.21 and a 12-month high of $29.85. The stock has a market capitalization of $1.41 billion, a P/E ratio of -2.69, a price-to-earnings-growth ratio of 0.55 and a beta of 1.22. The company has a current ratio of 1.05, a quick ratio of 0.87 and a debt-to-equity ratio of 0.65.

World Kinect ( NYSE:WKC - Get Free Report) last announced its quarterly earnings data on Thursday, April 23rd. The company reported $0.75 earnings per share (EPS) for the quarter, beating analysts' consensus estimates of $0.31 by $0.44. World Kinect had a positive return on equity of 8.27% and a negative net margin of 1.53%.The firm had revenue of $9.69 billion for the quarter, compared to analysts' expectations of $9.29 billion. During the same period last year, the firm earned $0.48 earnings per share. The company's revenue for the quarter was up 2.5% compared to the same quarter last year. World Kinect has set its FY 2026 guidance at 2.650-2.850 EPS. On average, equities analysts anticipate that World Kinect Corporation will post 2.83 earnings per share for the current year.

World Kinect Announces Dividend

The company also recently announced a quarterly dividend, which was paid on Thursday, April 16th. Stockholders of record on Monday, March 30th were issued a dividend of $0.20 per share. This represents a $0.80 dividend on an annualized basis and a dividend yield of 2.9%. The ex-dividend date was Monday, March 30th. World Kinect's dividend payout ratio is currently -7.84%.

Insider Transactions at World Kinect

In related news, Chairman Michael J. Kasbar sold 23,653 shares of the business's stock in a transaction dated Friday, April 24th. The stock was sold at an average price of $28.18, for a total transaction of $666,541.54. Following the transaction, the chairman owned 1,062,797 shares in the company, valued at $29,949,619.46. The trade was a 2.18% decrease in their position. The sale was disclosed in a document filed with the Securities & Exchange Commission, which is available at this link. 3.20% of the stock is currently owned by corporate insiders.

About World Kinect

( Free Report)

World Kinect Energy Services, Inc (NYSE: WKC) is a global energy services company specializing in fuel procurement, supply chain management and risk mitigation solutions. The company offers an integrated platform that facilitates the sourcing, trading and logistics of refined fuels, natural gas, liquefied natural gas (LNG) and renewable energy products. Its services are designed to help industrial, commercial and institutional clients optimize energy costs, comply with environmental regulations and manage price volatility.

In addition to traditional commodity trading and delivery, World Kinect provides a suite of value-added services that include carbon offset and decarbonization strategies, energy efficiency consulting and emissions reporting.

value.Title: CALIFORNIA ENERGY COMMISSION ISSUES SUBPOENA TO GOLDEN STATE WIND

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JH4-WVM3-S42N-83HH-00000-00CALIFORNIA ENERGY COMMISSION ISSUES SUBPOENA TO GOLDEN STATE WIND2026-05-04T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:40:07ZLexisNexisCALIFORNIA ENERGY COMMISSION ISSUES SUBPOENA TO GOLDEN STATE WIND

SACRAMENTO, Calif., May 4 -- The California Energy Commission issued the following news release:

WHAT YOU NEED TO KNOW:

The California Energy Commission has issued an administrative investigative subpoena to Golden State Wind regarding its deal with the Trump Administration to abandon an offshore wind project.

SACRAMENTO- Today, the California Energy Commission announced that it has issued an administrative investigative subpoenato Golden State Wind LLC seeking documents and information related to the company's recent agreement with the U.S. Department of the Interior to accept a payout in exchange for voluntarily abandoning its offshore wind lease.

"The Trump Administration is recklessly spending billions of taxpayer dollars on backroom deals that would turn back the clock on innovation" said CEC Chair David Hochschild. "Californians deserve immediate answers about the nature of this payout. Taxpayer dollars should be used to build a sustainable energy future, not to pay to make projects disappear."

According to the Department of the Interior, Golden State Wind has agreed to voluntarily relinquish its offshore wind lease located in the Morro Bay Wind Energy Area off the Central California coast (Lease OCS-P 0564) and forgo future offshore wind projects in the United States. In exchange, Golden State Wind will receive a $120 million payout from the federal government after making an equivalent investment in U.S. oil and gas projects outside of California.

At this time, no additional information has been made available to California or the public about the details of the lease buyout deal between Golden State Wind and the federal government. The CEC is requesting the agreement and other information from Golden State Wind, including the basis for the agreement and representations it made to the federal government during the development of this agreement, as part of an investigation.

The administrative investigative subpoena is available here.

###

About the California Energy Commission

The California Energy Commission is the state's primary energy policy and planning agency. It has seven core responsibilities: advancing state energy policy, encouraging energy efficiency, certifying power plants, investing in energy innovation, developing renewable energy, transforming transportation, and preparing for energy emergencies.

urn:contentItem:6JGS-W3T3-SHKP-S0PN-00000-00CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC - INDUSTRY BRIEF FROM PEERS2026-05-03T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:40:07ZLexisNexisSTOCKCLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC - INDUSTRY BRIEF FROM PEERS

DASHBOARD: CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC

PermID4296619307Websitehttp://www.clarkconstruction.comIndustryConstructionAddress18201 Von Karman Avenue Suite 800 Irvine, California 92612

ACTIVITIES:

Clark Construction Group, LLC, founded in 1906, is a leading general contractor headquartered in Bethesda, Maryland. With over 4,000 employees across 20+ offices, it serves commercial, infrastructure, and institutional sectors, delivering projects like stadiums, hospitals, and transit systems. Generating over $5 billion annually, Clark is known for its use of Building Information Modeling (BIM) and sustainable practices. In 2024, it completed major infrastructure projects, emphasizing green construction. Its culture prioritizes safety, diversity, and employee development, earning industry accolades. Competing with Turner Construction, Clark stands out for its large-scale project expertise and client partnerships. Recent initiatives include expanding renewable energy construction and digital tools for project management.

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC [4 Press Releases]

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC GREEN FROM THE INSIDE OUT: BERKELEY LAB SETS NEW STANDARDS

Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory's Biological and Environmental Program Integration Center sets new sustainability standards. Berkeley Lab Biological and Environmental Program Integration Center (BioEPIC) unites scientists from LBNL's biosciences and earth and environmental sciences areas in a four-story, 71,000-square-foot facility that sets a new standard for sustainability. Completed in March, the facility offers a suite of next-generation tools designed to study how microbial communities interact with plants and soil to influence the environment.

Inside, 200 researchers perform carefully controlled experiments on microbe-soil-plant interactions with instruments and computing infrastructure that connect BioEPIC to its field research sites. These unique climate research environments, along with three additional levels of labs and collaborative spaces, are housed in an all-electric facility designed to maximize energy efficiency and resiliency.

By supporting an array of unique research needs in this highly sustainable setting, the BioEPIC

Source: Company Website

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC BUILDERS AT HEART: WINSTON VAN KEULEN

This article is part of our series Builders at Heart, where we highlight the passions and backgrounds of the Clark team - the things that shape us - that allow us to tackle challenges head-on, solve complex problems, and build what matters.

Meet Winston van Keulen, a superintendent with Clark Construction, currently working in San Diego. Discover how he is progressing in his role and the keys to success he shares with new team members.

Source: Company Website

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC FROM CONCEPT TO REALITY: HARNESSING 3D PRINTING AT CLARK'S MID-ATLANTIC YARD

Clark Construction's Mid-Atlantic Yard in Upper Marlboro, Maryland, is a powerhouse of specialized services, from fleet oversight to custom fabrication. This diverse facility is also part of a transformative initiative: the integration of 3D printing for prototyping innovative custom solutions to solve complex challenges.

The method came to life when faced with a challenge: designing a custom cleaning piece for a drill rig auger. While the traditional route would involve costly and time-consuming steel fabrication for a prototype, Charles Harman III, a craft and machine shop supervisor, and his team explored a new solution for this type of fabrication and successfully used a 3D printer to create a scaled model. The successful prototype demonstrated the efficacy of the system, and once the 3D printed model was approved, the team began assembling the steel-fabricated version.

Source: Company Website

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC ENHANCING RICHMOND'S WATER RESILIENCE

Clark Water is currently leading improvements on the Shockoe Diversion Structure in Richmond - a key part of the city's combined sewer overflow (CSO) system, which was originally built in the early 1970s. The project will alleviate sewage and stormwater overflow during high-water events. To achieve this, crews have installed mechanical trash rake screens to prevent debris from passing through, two crest gates to minimize upstream flooding, and are making upgrades to supporting infrastructure.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION LABOR SHORTAGE LOOMS AS CONSTRUCTION REBOUNDS

SANTA CLARA, Calif., Dec. 9, 2025 - Just as individual market sectors are finding ways to move projects forward after a year of supply chain and cost turbulence, the ongoing labor shortage threatens to bog down construction unless owners and contractors embrace new approaches, according to the latest Market Conditions Report from DPR Construction, one of the nation's top technical builders.

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION [3 Press Releases]

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION HASLAM SPORTS GROUP, AECOM HUNT AND TURNER JV BREAK GROUND ON HUNTINGTON BANK FIELD

Construction of New State-of-the-Art Facility to Serve as Ohio's First Enclosed Stadium Revolutionary Design by HKS to Bring Football Fans Closer to the Field Than EverThe Haslam Sports Group (HSG) alongside a joint venture of AECOM Hunt and Turner Construction Company and HKS today announced the groundbreaking of the new Huntington Bank Field, the future home of the Cleveland Browns starting in the 2029 season. The groundbreaking ceremony held at the new site in Brook Park today marks the official start of construction on Northeast Ohio's largest economic development project to date, the new Huntington Bank Field enclosed stadium and surrounding district.

Source: Company Website

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION WHERE SMALL CHANGES CREATE REAL IMPACT

Turner's support to the Paraplegic Foundation reflects a focus on improving everyday spaces at Sanjeevandeep Special School. The initiative has contributed to creating a more accessible, comfortable environment for children and caregivers.Our recent association with the Paraplegic Foundation came from a shared belief that everyone deserves the opportunity to live with dignity, comfort, and as much independence as possible. Through their work, the Foundation supports initiatives like Sanjeevandeep Special School, a space dedicated to children with special needs, where care, learning, and rehabilitation come together in a very real, hands-on way.

Source: Company Website

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION: CONNECTING YOUNG TALENT WITH INDUSTRY LEADERS

As part of our Young Professionals Employee Resource Group initiatives, we hosted the first session of our Coffee Talks with Industry Leaders series, with the participation of Arup Turkiye Country Manager Tulay Hatirnaz, Associate Director Duygu Tokalakoglu, and Associate Aydan Basyazicioglu. Held on April 10 at the Arup Istanbul office, the session brought together our young professionals from across Turkiye. Our esteemed speakers shared their industry experience, career journeys, and leadership perspectives, while also engaging with participants through an interactive Q&A.

Source: Company Website

Clark Construction's Mid-Atlantic Yard in Upper Marlboro, Maryland, is a powerhouse of specialized services, from fleet oversight to custom fabrication. This diverse facility is also part of a transformative initiative: the integration of 3D printing for prototyping innovative custom solutions to solve complex challenges.

The method came to life when faced with a challenge: designing a custom cleaning piece for a drill rig auger. While the traditional route would involve costly and time-consuming steel fabrication for a prototype, Charles Harman III, a craft and machine shop supervisor, and his team explored a new solution for this type of fabrication and successfully used a 3D printer to create a scaled model. The successful prototype demonstrated the efficacy of the system, and once the 3D printed model was approved, the team began assembling the steel-fabricated version.

Source: Company Website

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC ENHANCING RICHMOND'S WATER RESILIENCE

Clark Water is currently leading improvements on the Shockoe Diversion Structure in Richmond - a key part of the city's combined sewer overflow (CSO) system, which was originally built in the early 1970s. The project will alleviate sewage and stormwater overflow during high-water events. To achieve this, crews have installed mechanical trash rake screens to prevent debris from passing through, two crest gates to minimize upstream flooding, and are making upgrades to supporting infrastructure.

Source: Company Website

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION [3 Press Releases]

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION HASLAM SPORTS GROUP, AECOM HUNT AND TURNER JV BREAK GROUND ON HUNTINGTON BANK FIELD

Construction of New State-of-the-Art Facility to Serve as Ohio's First Enclosed Stadium Revolutionary Design by HKS to Bring Football Fans Closer to the Field Than EverThe Haslam Sports Group (HSG) alongside a joint venture of AECOM Hunt and Turner Construction Company and HKS today announced the groundbreaking of the new Huntington Bank Field, the future home of the Cleveland Browns starting in the 2029 season. The groundbreaking ceremony held at the new site in Brook Park today marks the official start of construction on Northeast Ohio's largest economic development project to date, the new Huntington Bank Field enclosed stadium and surrounding district.

Source: Company Website

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION WHERE SMALL CHANGES CREATE REAL IMPACT

Turner's support to the Paraplegic Foundation reflects a focus on improving everyday spaces at Sanjeevandeep Special School. The initiative has contributed to creating a more accessible, comfortable environment for children and caregivers.Our recent association with the Paraplegic Foundation came from a shared belief that everyone deserves the opportunity to live with dignity, comfort, and as much independence as possible. Through their work, the Foundation supports initiatives like Sanjeevandeep Special School, a space dedicated to children with special needs, where care, learning, and rehabilitation come together in a very real, hands-on way.

Source: Company Website

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION: CONNECTING YOUNG TALENT WITH INDUSTRY LEADERS

As part of our Young Professionals Employee Resource Group initiatives, we hosted the first session of our Coffee Talks with Industry Leaders series, with the participation of Arup Turkiye Country Manager Tulay Hatirnaz, Associate Director Duygu Tokalakoglu, and Associate Aydan Basyazicioglu. Held on April 10 at the Arup Istanbul office, the session brought together our young professionals from across Turkiye. Our esteemed speakers shared their industry experience, career journeys, and leadership perspectives, while also engaging with participants through an interactive Q&A.

Source: Company Website

value.Title: ACT: CLEAN ENERGY LOWERS YOUR BILLS

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHD-GDV3-S5K0-Y28V-00000-00ACT: CLEAN ENERGY LOWERS YOUR BILLS2026-05-03T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:40:07ZLexisNexisACT: CLEAN ENERGY LOWERS YOUR BILLSStates News ServiceSAN FRANCISCO, CA

The following information was released by the Sierra Club:

Friend,

Across the country, families are feeling the strain of rising energy, fuel, and utility bills -- and are being forced to make tough choices. At the same time, energy demand is climbing fast due to the prolific growth of data centers and the digital economy. There is a clear, proven path forward with clean energy, but it's being deliberately blocked.

Renewable energy like wind and solar is already lowering costs, creating jobs, and delivering clean, reliable power to millions of homes.

That's why it's so alarming that the Trump administration is cutting backroom deals with fossil fuel billionaires instead of investing in solutions that will help lower our energy bills. In March, officials announced a nearly $1 billion agreement with TotalEnergies to cancel offshore wind projects off the coasts of North Carolina and New York -- projects that would have delivered affordable, clean power -- in exchange for expanding methane gas exports in Texas. And just this week, two more buy-outs were announced - deals that lock us into higher bills and more pollution, while putting taxpayers on the hook.

Trump is setting yet another dangerous precedent. If more companies walk away from their clean energy projects, it will stall progress, kill jobs, and drive energy prices even higher for families like yours -- especially as the growth of data centers drives more energy demand. These aren't just policy decisions -- they are choices that take affordable, reliable energy off the table for millions of families.

But here's the truth: clean energy is still winning.

Offshore wind projects are already delivering reliable power to the grid, proving that solutions are available now. In courtrooms across the country, judges are pushing back on unlawful attacks against clean energy, allowing projects to move forward and workers to get back on the job. Now, it's time for Congress to step in.

Call on Congress to investigate these backroom deals, protect wind and solar projects, and stand up for people forced to choose between keeping the lights on and putting food on the table.

We can't afford to let political deals drive up our energy bills. When we speak out, we can stop these backroom deals that benefit billionaires at our expense and build a future powered by clean energy that works for everyone.

In Solidarity,

Sierra Club

urn:contentItem:6JGW-R703-S0NX-0064-00000-00CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC - PEER INSIGHTS: INDUSTRY HIGHLIGHTS & KEY TRENDS2026-05-03T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:40:07ZLexisNexisSTOCKCLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC - PEER INSIGHTS: INDUSTRY HIGHLIGHTS & KEY TRENDS

DASHBOARD: CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC

PermID4296619307Websitehttp://www.clarkconstruction.comIndustryConstructionAddress18201 Von Karman Avenue Suite 800 Irvine, California 92612

ACTIVITIES:

Clark Construction Group, LLC, founded in 1906, is a leading general contractor headquartered in Bethesda, Maryland. With over 4,000 employees across 20+ offices, it serves commercial, infrastructure, and institutional sectors, delivering projects like stadiums, hospitals, and transit systems. Generating over $5 billion annually, Clark is known for its use of Building Information Modeling (BIM) and sustainable practices. In 2024, it completed major infrastructure projects, emphasizing green construction. Its culture prioritizes safety, diversity, and employee development, earning industry accolades. Competing with Turner Construction, Clark stands out for its large-scale project expertise and client partnerships. Recent initiatives include expanding renewable energy construction and digital tools for project management.

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC [4 Press Releases]

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC GREEN FROM THE INSIDE OUT: BERKELEY LAB SETS NEW STANDARDS

Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory's Biological and Environmental Program Integration Center sets new sustainability standards. Berkeley Lab Biological and Environmental Program Integration Center (BioEPIC) unites scientists from LBNL's biosciences and earth and environmental sciences areas in a four-story, 71,000-square-foot facility that sets a new standard for sustainability. Completed in March, the facility offers a suite of next-generation tools designed to study how microbial communities interact with plants and soil to influence the environment.

Inside, 200 researchers perform carefully controlled experiments on microbe-soil-plant interactions with instruments and computing infrastructure that connect BioEPIC to its field research sites. These unique climate research environments, along with three additional levels of labs and collaborative spaces, are housed in an all-electric facility designed to maximize energy efficiency and resiliency.

By supporting an array of unique research needs in this highly sustainable setting, the BioEPIC

Source: Company Website

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC BUILDERS AT HEART: WINSTON VAN KEULEN

This article is part of our series Builders at Heart, where we highlight the passions and backgrounds of the Clark team - the things that shape us - that allow us to tackle challenges head-on, solve complex problems, and build what matters.

Meet Winston van Keulen, a superintendent with Clark Construction, currently working in San Diego. Discover how he is progressing in his role and the keys to success he shares with new team members.

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC FROM CONCEPT TO REALITY: HARNESSING 3D PRINTING AT CLARK'S MID-ATLANTIC YARD

Clark Construction's Mid-Atlantic Yard in Upper Marlboro, Maryland, is a powerhouse of specialized services, from fleet oversight to custom fabrication. This diverse facility is also part of a transformative initiative: the integration of 3D printing for prototyping innovative custom solutions to solve complex challenges.

The method came to life when faced with a challenge: designing a custom cleaning piece for a drill rig auger. While the traditional route would involve costly and time-consuming steel fabrication for a prototype, Charles Harman III, a craft and machine shop supervisor, and his team explored a new solution for this type of fabrication and successfully used a 3D printer to create a scaled model. The successful prototype demonstrated the efficacy of the system, and once the 3D printed model was approved, the team began assembling the steel-fabricated version.

Source: Company Website

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC ENHANCING RICHMOND'S WATER RESILIENCE

Clark Water is currently leading improvements on the Shockoe Diversion Structure in Richmond - a key part of the city's combined sewer overflow (CSO) system, which was originally built in the early 1970s. The project will alleviate sewage and stormwater overflow during high-water events.
